30	@api_view(["GET", "POST"])
31	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsComplianceOrAdmin])
32	def subscription_list_create(request):
33	    """
34	    GET  /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/  -- list (compliance/admin only)
35	    POST /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/  -- create (admin only)
36	    """
37	    if request.method == "GET":
38	        subscriptions = ReportSubscription.objects.select_related("created_by").all()
39	
40	        page = int(request.query_params.get("page", 1))
41	        page_size = int(request.query_params.get("page_size", 20))
42	        page_size = min(page_size, 100)
43	        start = (page - 1) * page_size
44	        end = start + page_size
45	        total = subscriptions.count()
46	
47	        serializer = ReportSubscriptionListSerializer(
48	            subscriptions[start:end], many=True
49	        )
50	        return Response({
51	            "count": total,
52	            "next": None,
53	            "previous": None,
54	            "results": serializer.data,
55	        })
56	
57	    # POST -- admin only.
58	    if not IsAdmin().has_permission(request, None):
59	        return Response(
60	            {
61	                "error": "permission_denied",
62	                "message": "Administrator privileges required to create subscriptions.",
63	                "status_code": 403,
64	            },
65	            status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
66	        )
67	
68	    serializer = ReportSubscriptionCreateSerializer(data=request.data)
69	    serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)
70	    subscription = serializer.save(created_by=request.user)
71	
72	    request._audit_context = {
73	        "event_type": "create",
74	        "target_model": "ReportSubscription",
75	        "target_id": str(subscription.pk),
76	        "target_repr": subscription.name,
77	    }
78	
79	    return Response(
80	        ReportSubscriptionListSerializer(subscription).data,
81	        status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
82	    )
83	
84	
85	@api_view(["GET", "PATCH", "DELETE"])
86	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsComplianceOrAdmin])
87	def subscription_detail(request, pk):
88	    """
89	    GET    /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/{id}/
90	    PATCH  /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/{id}/  (admin or creator only)
91	    DELETE /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/{id}/  (admin or creator only -- soft deactivate)
92	    """
93	    try:
94	        subscription = ReportSubscription.objects.select_related("created_by").get(pk=pk)
95	    except ReportSubscription.DoesNotExist:
96	        return Response(
97	            {
98	                "error": "not_found",
99	                "message": "Report subscription not found.",
100	                "status_code": 404,
101	            },
102	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
103	        )
104	
105	    if request.method == "GET":
106	        return Response(ReportSubscriptionListSerializer(subscription).data)
107	
108	    # PATCH / DELETE require admin or the creator of the subscription.
109	    is_admin = request.user.role == "admin"
110	    is_creator = (
111	        subscription.created_by_id is not None
112	        and subscription.created_by_id == request.user.pk
113	    )
114	    if not (is_admin or is_creator):
115	        return Response(
116	            {
117	                "error": "permission_denied",
118	                "message": "Administrator privileges or subscription ownership required.",
119	                "status_code": 403,
120	            },
121	            status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
122	        )
123	
124	    if request.method == "PATCH":
125	        serializer = ReportSubscriptionCreateSerializer(
126	            subscription, data=request.data, partial=True
127	        )
128	        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)
129	        serializer.save()
130	
131	        request._audit_context = {
132	            "event_type": "update",
133	            "target_model": "ReportSubscription",
134	            "target_id": str(subscription.pk),
135	            "target_repr": subscription.name,
136	            "field_changes": request.data,
137	        }
138	
139	        return Response(ReportSubscriptionListSerializer(subscription).data)
140	
141	    # DELETE -- soft-deactivate.
142	    subscription.is_active = False
143	    subscription.save(update_fields=["is_active", "updated_at"])
144	
145	    request._audit_context = {
146	        "event_type": "update",
147	        "target_model": "ReportSubscription",
148	        "target_id": str(subscription.pk),
149	        "target_repr": f"Deactivated: {subscription.name}",
150	    }
151	
152	    return Response({"message": "Subscription deactivated."})
153	
154	
155	@api_view(["POST"])
156	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsAdmin])
157	def subscription_run_now(request, pk):
158	    """
159	    POST /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/{id}/run-now/
160	
161	    Trigger immediate report generation. Admin only.
162	    """
163	    try:
164	        subscription = ReportSubscription.objects.get(pk=pk)
165	    except ReportSubscription.DoesNotExist:
166	        return Response(
167	            {
168	                "error": "not_found",
169	                "message": "Report subscription not found.",
170	                "status_code": 404,
171	            },
172	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
173	        )
174	
175	    # Determine file format based on output_format.
176	    format_map = {"pdf": "pdf", "excel": "xlsx", "image": "png"}
177	    file_format = format_map.get(subscription.output_format, "pdf")
178	
179	    outbox_item = OutboxItem.objects.create(
180	        subscription=subscription,
181	        report_name=f"{subscription.name} (manual run)",
182	        file_format=file_format,
183	        status=OutboxStatus.QUEUED,
184	        delivery_target=subscription.parameters.get("delivery_target", "shared_folder"),
185	        delivery_target_path=subscription.parameters.get("delivery_path", ""),
186	    )
187	
188	    # Trigger async generation.
189	    try:
190	        from .tasks import generate_report
191	        generate_report.delay(str(outbox_item.pk))
192	    except Exception:
193	        logger.exception("Failed to enqueue generate_report task")
194	
195	    request._audit_context = {
196	        "event_type": "report_generated",
197	        "target_model": "ReportSubscription",
198	        "target_id": str(subscription.pk),
199	        "target_repr": f"Manual run: {subscription.name}",
200	        "extra_data": {"outbox_item_id": str(outbox_item.pk)},
201	    }
202	
203	    return Response(
204	        {
205	            "message": "Report generation queued.",
206	            "outbox_item": OutboxItemSerializer(outbox_item).data,
207	        },
208	        status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
209	    )

237	    end = start + page_size

275	@api_view(["GET"])
276	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsComplianceOrAdmin])
277	def outbox_download(request, pk):
278	    """
279	    GET /api/v1/reports/outbox/{id}/download/
280	
281	    Serve the generated report file for download.
282	    Admin and compliance only.
283	    """
284	    try:
285	        item = OutboxItem.objects.get(pk=pk)
286	    except OutboxItem.DoesNotExist:
287	        return Response(
288	            {
289	                "error": "not_found",
290	                "message": "Outbox item not found.",
291	                "status_code": 404,
292	            },
293	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
294	        )
295	
296	    if not item.file_path:
297	        return Response(
298	            {
299	                "error": "file_not_ready",
300	                "message": "Report file has not been generated yet.",
301	                "status_code": 404,
302	            },
303	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
304	        )
305	
306	    storage_root = getattr(settings, "MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT", settings.MEDIA_ROOT)
307	    full_path = os.path.join(storage_root, item.file_path)
308	
309	    if not os.path.isfile(full_path):
310	        return Response(
311	            {
312	                "error": "file_missing",
313	                "message": "Report file not found on disk.",
314	                "status_code": 404,
315	            },
316	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
317	        )
318	
319	    content_types = {
320	        "pdf": "application/pdf",
321	        "xlsx": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet",
322	        "png": "image/png",
323	    }
324	    content_type = content_types.get(item.file_format, "application/octet-stream")
325	    filename = os.path.basename(full_path)
326	
327	    return FileResponse(
328	        open(full_path, "rb"),
329	        content_type=content_type,
330	        as_attachment=True,
331	        filename=filename,
332	    )

349	                "message": "Outbox item not found.",
350	                "status_code": 404,
351	            },
352	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
353	        )
354	
355	    if item.status not in (OutboxStatus.FAILED, OutboxStatus.STALLED):
356	        return Response(
357	            {
358	                "error": "invalid_status",
359	                "message": f"Cannot retry item with status '{item.status}'. Only failed or stalled items can be retried.",
360	                "status_code": 400,
361	            },
362	            status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
363	        )
364	
365	    item.status = OutboxStatus.QUEUED
366	    item.last_error = ""
367	    item.next_retry_at = None
368	    item.save(update_fields=["status", "last_error", "next_retry_at"])
369	
370	    # Trigger async delivery.
371	    try:
372	        from .tasks import deliver_outbox_item
373	        deliver_outbox_item.delay(str(item.pk))
374	    except Exception:
375	        logger.exception("Failed to enqueue deliver_outbox_item task")
376	
377	    request._audit_context = {
378	        "event_type": "report_delivered",
379	        "target_model": "OutboxItem",
380	        "target_id": str(item.pk),
381	        "target_repr": f"Manual retry: {item.report_name}",
382	    }
383	
384	    return Response({
385	        "message": "Retry queued.",
386	        "outbox_item": OutboxItemSerializer(item).data,
387	    })
388	
389	
390	@api_view(["POST"])
391	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsAdmin])
392	def outbox_acknowledge(request, pk):
393	    """
394	    POST /api/v1/reports/outbox/{id}/acknowledge/
395	
396	    Acknowledge a stalled outbox item. Admin only.
397	    """
398	    try:
399	        item = OutboxItem.objects.get(pk=pk)
400	    except OutboxItem.DoesNotExist:
401	        return Response(
402	            {
403	                "error": "not_found",
404	                "message": "Outbox item not found.",
405	                "status_code": 404,
406	            },
407	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
408	        )
409	
410	    if item.status != OutboxStatus.STALLED:
411	        return Response(
412	            {
413	                "error": "invalid_status",
414	                "message": "Only stalled items can be acknowledged.",
415	                "status_code": 400,
416	            },
417	            status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
418	        )
419	
420	    item.acknowledged_by = request.user
421	    item.acknowledged_at = timezone.now()
422	    item.save(update_fields=["acknowledged_by", "acknowledged_at"])
423	
424	    request._audit_context = {
425	        "event_type": "report_delivered",
426	        "target_model": "OutboxItem",
427	        "target_id": str(item.pk),
428	        "target_repr": f"Acknowledged: {item.report_name}",
429	    }
430	
431	    return Response({
432	        "message": "Outbox item acknowledged.",
433	        "outbox_item": OutboxItemSerializer(item).data,
434	    })
435	
436	
437	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
438	# Dashboard
439	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
440	
441	@api_view(["GET"])
442	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsComplianceOrAdmin])
443	def report_dashboard(request):
444	    """
445	    GET /api/v1/reports/dashboard/
446	
447	    Counts by outbox status plus recent items.
448	    Admin and compliance only.
449	    """
450	    from django.db.models import Count, Q
451	
452	    aggregates = OutboxItem.objects.aggregate(
453	        queued=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.QUEUED)),
454	        generating=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.GENERATING)),
455	        delivered=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.DELIVERED)),
456	        failed=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.FAILED)),
457	        stalled=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.STALLED)),
458	        total=Count("id"),
459	    )
460	
461	    recent_items = OutboxItem.objects.select_related(
462	        "subscription", "acknowledged_by"
463	    ).order_by("-generated_at")[:10]
464	
465	    dashboard_serializer = OutboxDashboardSerializer(aggregates)
466	
467	    return Response({
468	        "queued": aggregates["queued"],
469	        "generating": aggregates["generating"],
470	        "delivered": aggregates["delivered"],
471	        "failed": aggregates["failed"],
472	        "stalled": aggregates["stalled"],
473	        "recent": OutboxItemSerializer(recent_items, many=True).data,
474	    })

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary:

---

## Project Structure Overview

**Overall Architecture:**
- **Backend:** Django REST Framework application with multiple microservice-like apps
- **Frontend:** React + TypeScript with modern tooling (Vite)
- **Infrastructure:** Docker-based setup with middleware, services, and repositories

**Directory Structure:**
```
repo/
├── backend/
│   ├── apps/
│   │   ├── accounts/        # User management, auth, permissions
│   │   ├── audit/           # Audit logging and verification
│   │   ├── consent/         # Patient consent records
│   │   ├── financials/      # Orders, payments, reconciliation
│   │   ├── health/          # Health checks
│   │   ├── media_engine/    # Media management & originality checking
│   │   ├── mpi/             # Master Patient Index
│   │   └── reports/         # Report generation and subscriptions
│   ├── domain/              # Business logic, services, validators
│   ├── infrastructure/      # Middleware, encryption, logging, repositories
│   └── medrights/           # Django settings, URLs, WSGI
├── frontend/                # React + TypeScript UI
├── e2e/                     # End-to-end tests
└── docker-compose.yml       # Docker orchestration
```

---

## File Contents (Full)

### 1. **backend/apps/media_engine/views.py** (649 lines)

Handles media asset management with comprehensive originality checking and copyright protection:

**Key Endpoints:**
- `media_upload()` - Upload media with fingerprinting and originality check
- `media_list()` - List media assets with filtering by patient, status, search
- `media_detail()` - Get full details of a media asset
- `media_download()` - Download media files with consent/repost authorization checks
- `media_watermark()` - Apply server-side watermark to media
- `repost_authorize()` - Attach citation and authorization for reposted media
- `media_attach_patient()` - Link media to a patient
- `infringement_list_create()` - Create/list copyright infringement reports
- `infringement_detail_update()` - Update infringement status with state machine validation

**Key Features:**
- Consent revocation checks before download
- Repost authorization validation (requires citation + authorization file)
- Media originality status transitions (original → reposted → disputed → resolved)
- Audit context set for all state-changing operations
- Transaction-based operations for atomicity

---

### 2. **backend/apps/media_engine/serializers.py** (435 lines)

DRF serializers for media operations:

**Serializer Classes:**
- `MediaUploadSerializer` - Validates file type/size, computes pixel & file hashes, checks originality, extracts metadata
- `MediaDetailSerializer` - Full media details with computed `repost_authorized` field
- `MediaListSerializer` - Lightweight list view
- `WatermarkSerializer` - Watermark config (opacity, clinic name, date stamp)
- `RepostAuthorizeSerializer` - Citation text + authorization file
- `CitationSerializer` - Citation records (read-only)
- `InfringementCreateSerializer` - Create infringement reports with screenshot storage
- `InfringementDetailSerializer` - Full infringement report details
- `InfringementListSerializer` - Lightweight infringement list
- `InfringementUpdateSerializer` - Status updates with state machine validation using `validate_infringement_transition()`

**Key Logic:**
- Max upload size: 20 MB
- File storage at `storage/media/YYYY/MM/UUID.ext`
- Pixel hash computation for originality detection
- Evidence metadata extraction (immutable)
- Watermarked derivatives stored separately with derivative hashing

---

### 3. **backend/apps/consent/models.py** (105 lines)

Consent and scope models:

**Models:**
- `Consent` - Patient consent records with:
  - `id` (UUID primary key)
  - `patient` (FK to Patient)
  - `purpose`, `effective_date`, `expiration_date`
  - `is_revoked`, `revoked_at`, `revoked_by`, `revocation_reason`
  - `physical_copy_on_file` - tracks if consent exists in physical form
  - `granted_by` (FK to User)
  - Computed `status` property: 'active', 'expired', or 'revoked'

- `ConsentScope` - Fine-grained scope entries:
  - Scope types: data_field, action, media_use, third_party
  - Related to Consent via FK with CASCADE delete

**Key Features:**
- Indefinite consent support (null expiration_date)
- Physical copy tracking for compliance
- Hash-chained audit integration via middleware

---

### 4. **backend/apps/consent/serializers.py** (155 lines)

Consent serializers for CRUD operations:

**Serializer Classes:**
- `ConsentScopeSerializer` - Scope fields with read-only IDs
- `ConsentCreateSerializer` - Create consent with validation:
  - Validates date ranges via `validate_consent_dates()`
  - Creates scopes in atomic transaction
  - Returns data via `ConsentListSerializer`

- `ConsentListSerializer` - Read-only summary with computed status
- `ConsentRevokeSerializer` - Revoke consent with:
  - Reason (required)
  - Physical copy warning acknowledgment
  - Validates revocation via `validate_revocation()`
  - Updates `is_revoked`, `revoked_at`, `revoked_by`, `revocation_reason`

**Domain Service Integration:**
- Uses `compute_consent_status()` for status computation
- Uses `validate_consent_dates()` for effective/expiration validation
- Uses `validate_revocation()` for revocation business rules

---

### 5. **backend/apps/financials/tasks.py** (218 lines)

Celery tasks for financial operations:

**Tasks:**

1. **`auto_close_unpaid_orders()`**
   - Runs every 60 seconds via Celery Beat
   - Closes orders past their `auto_close_at` deadline
   - Idempotent operation
   - Returns count of closed orders

2. **`generate_daily_reconciliation(target_date=None)`**
   - Generates end-of-day reconciliation reports
   - Aggregates orders, payments, refunds by date
   - Idempotency: checks if reconciliation already exists for date
   - Generates CSV with:
     - Summary row: date, total orders, revenue, payments, refunds, discrepancy
     - Order detail section
     - Payment detail section
   - Detects deferred reconciliations (generated days later)
   - Stores CSV in `storage/reconciliation/`

3. **`check_deferred_reconciliation()`**
   - Called on user login to catch up after offline periods
   - Checks last 7 days for missing reconciliation records
   - Regenerates missing reports
   - Returns list of generated dates

**Data Aggregation:**
- Orders: count, sum(total_amount)
- Payments: sum(amount) by date
- Refunds: sum(amount) where status='completed' by date
- Discrepancy calculation: total_payments - total_refunds - total_revenue

---

### 6. **backend/infrastructure/middleware/audit_logging.py** (52 lines)

Audit logging middleware:

**Functionality:**
- Captures all HTTP requests (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, etc.)
- After response processing, checks for `request._audit_context`
- Calls `create_audit_entry()` if context is populated
- Works for all HTTP methods (views opt-in by setting context)

**Flow:**
1. Initialize empty `_audit_context` on all requests
2. Process request through view
3. After response, check if `_audit_context` has `event_type`
4. Create audit entry with extracted context data

**Error Handling:**
- Logs exception if audit entry creation fails
- Does not block response on audit failure

---

### 7. **backend/apps/audit/service.py** (218 lines)

Hash-chained, tamper-evident audit logging service:

**Core Functions:**

1. **`_canonical_json(data: dict) -> str`**
   - Deterministic JSON serialization (sort_keys=True)

2. **`_compute_hash(entry_data: dict, previous_hash: str) -> str`**
   - Computes SHA-256(canonical_json(entry_data) + previous_hash)
   - Forms verifiable hash chain

3. **`create_audit_entry(...)`**
   - Atomically creates hash-chained audit entries
   - Uses SELECT FOR UPDATE lock on last entry to prevent concurrent write conflicts
   - Parameters:
     - event_type, user, username_snapshot, client_ip, workstation_id
     - target_model, target_id, target_repr
     - field_changes ({"field": {"old": ..., "new": ...}})
     - extra_data
   - Returns newly created AuditEntry
   - Always logs event even if DB write fails

4. **`verify_audit_chain() -> tuple[bool, int | None, int]`**
   - Walks entire audit chain verifying every entry's hash
   - Handles gaps from archived (purged) segments
   - Consults AuditArchiveSegment for boundary hashes
   - Returns (is_valid, broken_at_id, total_checked)

**Chain Verification:**
- Uses GENESIS_HASH as starting point
- Expected_previous_hash incremented for each entry
- Detects tampering if recomputed hash != entry_hash

---

### 8. **backend/apps/audit/views_client_logs.py** (193 lines)

Client-side error logging endpoint for JavaScript errors:

**Endpoint:**
- `client_error_log()` - POST /api/v1/logs/client-error/
- Accepts unauthenticated requests (AllowAny)
- Rate limited: 10 requests/minute per IP

**Request Payload Validation:**

1. **Origin/Referer Check:**
   - Validates exact scheme+host+port match against CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS
   - Rejects untrusted origins
   - Rejects missing origin/referer for unauthenticated requests

2. **Payload Size:**
   - Max 10 KB (MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES)

3. **Field Validation:**
   - Allowlist: {message, stack, url, timestamp, component, level, user_agent, extra}
   - Rejects unknown fields
   - Rejects sensitive field names

4. **Sensitive Field Detection:**
   - Keywords: {password, token, secret, key, ssn, dob, mrn} (case-insensitive)
   - Strips sensitive keys from 'extra' dict
   - Rejects if any top-level field contains sensitive keyword

**Field Limits:**
- message: 2000 chars
- stack: 5000 chars
- url, user_agent: 500 chars
- timestamp, component: 100, 200 chars respectively

**Response:**
- Returns 201 CREATED with {"status": "logged"}
- Binds authenticated user to log if session exists
- Logs to "medrights.audit" logger with extra context

---

### 9. **backend/apps/reports/views.py** (475 lines)

Reports and outbox management views:

**Subscription Management:**

1. **`subscription_list_create(request)`**
   - GET: List subscriptions (compliance/admin)
   - POST: Create subscription (admin only)
   - Pagination: page, page_size (max 100)

2. **`subscription_detail(request, pk)`**
   - GET: Retrieve subscription
   - PATCH: Update (admin or creator only)
   - DELETE: Soft-deactivate (admin or creator only)

3. **`subscription_run_now(request, pk)`**
   - POST: Trigger immediate report generation (admin only)
   - Creates OutboxItem with QUEUED status
   - Enqueues async `generate_report` task

**Outbox Management:**

4. **`outbox_list(request)`**
   - GET: List outbox items with optional status filter
   - Status values from OutboxStatus enum
   - Pagination: page, page_size (max 100)

5. **`outbox_detail(request, pk)`**
   - GET: Retrieve single outbox item

6. **`outbox_download(request, pk)`**
   - GET: Serve generated report file
   - Content-type mapping: pdf, xlsx, png
   - Returns FileResponse with proper headers

7. **`outbox_retry(request, pk)`**
   - POST: Manually retry failed/stalled items (admin only)
   - Resets status to QUEUED
   - Clears error message
   - Enqueues async `deliver_outbox_item` task

8. **`outbox_acknowledge(request, pk)`**
   - POST: Acknowledge stalled items (admin only)
   - Sets acknowledged_by and acknowledged_at

**Dashboard:**

9. **`report_dashboard(request)`**
   - GET: Summary counts and recent items
   - Aggregates by status: queued, generating, delivered, failed, stalled
   - Returns 10 most recent items by generated_at

**Key Features:**
- File format mapping: pdf, xlsx, png
- Storage root: MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT or MEDIA_ROOT
- All endpoints set `request._audit_context` for audit logging
- Pagination with configurable page size

---

### 10. **frontend/src/features/patients/components/ConsentCard.tsx** (216 lines)

React component for displaying and revoking patient consent:

**Component Props:**
```typescript
interface ConsentCardProps {
  consent: Consent;
  patientId: string;
}
```

**Features:**

1. **Status Badge:**
   - Color-coded by status: active (green), expired (gray), revoked (red)
   - Displays Consent.status property

2. **Consent Details Display:**
   - Purpose, effective_date, expiration_date (with fallback "No expiration")
   - Granted timestamp, revocation timestamp (if revoked)
   - Physical copy warning badge if on file

3. **Revocation Dialog:**
   - Only shows when consent.status === "active"
   - Modal with backdrop
   - Form fields:
     - Reason for revocation (required, textarea)
     - Physical copy acknowledgment checkbox (if consent.physical_copy_on_file)
   - Disables submit if:
     - Mutation pending
     - Reason empty
     - Physical copy checkbox unchecked (if applicable)

4. **State Management:**
   - `revokeDialogOpen` - Modal visibility
   - `revokeReason` - Textarea value
   - `physicalAcknowledged` - Checkbox state

5. **Mutation:**
   - Calls `revokeConsent(patientId, consent.id, { reason, physical_copy_warning_acknowledged })`
   - On success:
     - Shows toast notification
     - Closes dialog, resets form
     - Invalidates react-query cache for ["consents", patientId]
   - On error:
     - Shows error toast

**UI Elements:**
- Lucide icons: FileText (document), AlertTriangle (warning), X (close), Loader2 (spinner)
- Tailwind CSS with dark mode support
- Responsive grid layout (sm:grid-cols-2)
- Custom button styles with disabled states

---

## Summary

This is a comprehensive medical records management system with:

- **Media Management**: Originality detection, watermarking, copyright infringement tracking
- **Consent Management**: Patient consent lifecycle with revocation support
- **Financial Operations**: Order management, payment processing, daily reconciliation
- **Audit Logging**: Hash-chained, tamper-evident audit trail for compliance
- **Reporting**: Subscription-based report generation and delivery
- **Client Error Tracking**: JavaScript error logging with sensitive field protection

All backend operations include audit context integration for compliance tracking, and the system emphasizes data integrity through hash-chained audit logs and transaction management.

- Evidence metadata extraction (immutable)
- Watermarked derivatives stored separately with derivative hashing

---

### 3. **backend/apps/consent/models.py** (105 lines)

Consent and scope models:

**Models:**
- `Consent` - Patient consent records with:
  - `id` (UUID primary key)
  - `patient` (FK to Patient)
  - `purpose`, `effective_date`, `expiration_date`
  - `is_revoked`, `revoked_at`, `revoked_by`, `revocation_reason`
  - `physical_copy_on_file` - tracks if consent exists in physical form
  - `granted_by` (FK to User)
  - Computed `status` property: 'active', 'expired', or 'revoked'

- `ConsentScope` - Fine-grained scope entries:
  - Scope types: data_field, action, media_use, third_party
  - Related to Consent via FK with CASCADE delete

**Key Features:**
- Indefinite consent support (null expiration_date)
- Physical copy tracking for compliance
- Hash-chained audit integration via middleware

---

### 4. **backend/apps/consent/serializers.py** (155 lines)

Consent serializers for CRUD operations:

**Serializer Classes:**
- `ConsentScopeSerializer` - Scope fields with read-only IDs
- `ConsentCreateSerializer` - Create consent with validation:
  - Validates date ranges via `validate_consent_dates()`
  - Creates scopes in atomic transaction
  - Returns data via `ConsentListSerializer`

- `ConsentListSerializer` - Read-only summary with computed status
- `ConsentRevokeSerializer` - Revoke consent with:
  - Reason (required)
  - Physical copy warning acknowledgment
  - Validates revocation via `validate_revocation()`
  - Updates `is_revoked`, `revoked_at`, `revoked_by`, `revocation_reason`

**Domain Service Integration:**
- Uses `compute_consent_status()` for status computation
- Uses `validate_consent_dates()` for effective/expiration validation
- Uses `validate_revocation()` for revocation business rules

---

### 5. **backend/apps/financials/tasks.py** (218 lines)

Celery tasks for financial operations:

**Tasks:**

1. **`auto_close_unpaid_orders()`**
   - Runs every 60 seconds via Celery Beat
   - Closes orders past their `auto_close_at` deadline
   - Idempotent operation
   - Returns count of closed orders

2. **`generate_daily_reconciliation(target_date=None)`**
   - Generates end-of-day reconciliation reports
   - Aggregates orders, payments, refunds by date
   - Idempotency: checks if reconciliation already exists for date
   - Generates CSV with:
     - Summary row: date, total orders, revenue, payments, refunds, discrepancy
     - Order detail section
     - Payment detail section
   - Detects deferred reconciliations (generated days later)
   - Stores CSV in `storage/reconciliation/`

3. **`check_deferred_reconciliation()`**
   - Called on user login to catch up after offline periods
   - Checks last 7 days for missing reconciliation records
   - Regenerates missing reports
   - Returns list of generated dates

**Data Aggregation:**
- Orders: count, sum(total_amount)
- Payments: sum(amount) by date
- Refunds: sum(amount) where status='completed' by date
- Discrepancy calculation: total_payments - total_refunds - total_revenue

---

### 6. **backend/infrastructure/middleware/audit_logging.py** (52 lines)

Audit logging middleware:

**Functionality:**
- Captures all HTTP requests (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, etc.)
- After response processing, checks for `request._audit_context`
- Calls `create_audit_entry()` if context is populated
- Works for all HTTP methods (views opt-in by setting context)

**Flow:**
1. Initialize empty `_audit_context` on all requests
2. Process request through view
3. After response, check if `_audit_context` has `event_type`
4. Create audit entry with extracted context data

**Error Handling:**
- Logs exception if audit entry creation fails
- Does not block response on audit failure

---

### 7. **backend/apps/audit/service.py** (218 lines)

Hash-chained, tamper-evident audit logging service:

**Core Functions:**

1. **`_canonical_json(data: dict) -> str`**
   - Deterministic JSON serialization (sort_keys=True)

2. **`_compute_hash(entry_data: dict, previous_hash: str) -> str`**
   - Computes SHA-256(canonical_json(entry_data) + previous_hash)
   - Forms verifiable hash chain

3. **`create_audit_entry(...)`**
   - Atomically creates hash-chained audit entries
   - Uses SELECT FOR UPDATE lock on last entry to prevent concurrent write conflicts
   - Parameters:
     - event_type, user, username_snapshot, client_ip, workstation_id
     - target_model, target_id, target_repr
     - field_changes ({"field": {"old": ..., "new": ...}})
     - extra_data
   - Returns newly created AuditEntry
   - Always logs event even if DB write fails

4. **`verify_audit_chain() -> tuple[bool, int | None, int]`**
   - Walks entire audit chain verifying every entry's hash
   - Handles gaps from archived (purged) segments
   - Consults AuditArchiveSegment for boundary hashes
   - Returns (is_valid, broken_at_id, total_checked)

**Chain Verification:**
- Uses GENESIS_HASH as starting point
- Expected_previous_hash incremented for each entry
- Detects tampering if recomputed hash != entry_hash

---

### 8. **backend/apps/audit/views_client_logs.py** (193 lines)

Client-side error logging endpoint for JavaScript errors:

**Endpoint:**
- `client_error_log()` - POST /api/v1/logs/client-error/
- Accepts unauthenticated requests (AllowAny)
- Rate limited: 10 requests/minute per IP

**Request Payload Validation:**

1. **Origin/Referer Check:**
   - Validates exact scheme+host+port match against CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS
   - Rejects untrusted origins
   - Rejects missing origin/referer for unauthenticated requests

2. **Payload Size:**
   - Max 10 KB (MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES)

3. **Field Validation:**
   - Allowlist: {message, stack, url, timestamp, component, level, user_agent, extra}
   - Rejects unknown fields
   - Rejects sensitive field names

4. **Sensitive Field Detection:**
   - Keywords: {password, token, secret, key, ssn, dob, mrn} (case-insensitive)
   - Strips sensitive keys from 'extra' dict
   - Rejects if any top-level field contains sensitive keyword

**Field Limits:**
- message: 2000 chars
- stack: 5000 chars
- url, user_agent: 500 chars
- timestamp, component: 100, 200 chars respectively

**Response:**
- Returns 201 CREATED with {"status": "logged"}
- Binds authenticated user to log if session exists
- Logs to "medrights.audit" logger with extra context

---

### 9. **backend/apps/reports/views.py** (475 lines)

Reports and outbox management views:

**Subscription Management:**

1. **`subscription_list_create(request)`**
   - GET: List subscriptions (compliance/admin)
   - POST: Create subscription (admin only)
   - Pagination: page, page_size (max 100)

2. **`subscription_detail(request, pk)`**
   - GET: Retrieve subscription
   - PATCH: Update (admin or creator only)
   - DELETE: Soft-deactivate (admin or creator only)

3. **`subscription_run_now(request, pk)`**
   - POST: Trigger immediate report generation (admin only)
   - Creates OutboxItem with QUEUED status
   - Enqueues async `generate_report` task

**Outbox Management:**

4. **`outbox_list(request)`**
   - GET: List outbox items with optional status filter
   - Status values from OutboxStatus enum
   - Pagination: page, page_size (max 100)

5. **`outbox_detail(request, pk)`**
   - GET: Retrieve single outbox item

6. **`outbox_download(request, pk)`**
   - GET: Serve generated report file
   - Content-type mapping: pdf, xlsx, png
   - Returns FileResponse with proper headers

7. **`outbox_retry(request, pk)`**
   - POST: Manually retry failed/stalled items (admin only)
   - Resets status to QUEUED
   - Clears error message
   - Enqueues async `deliver_outbox_item` task

8. **`outbox_acknowledge(request, pk)`**
   - POST: Acknowledge stalled items (admin only)
   - Sets acknowledged_by and acknowledged_at

**Dashboard:**

9. **`report_dashboard(request)`**
   - GET: Summary counts and recent items
   - Aggregates by status: queued, generating, delivered, failed, stalled
   - Returns 10 most recent items by generated_at

**Key Features:**
- File format mapping: pdf, xlsx, png
- Storage root: MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT or MEDIA_ROOT
- All endpoints set `request._audit_context` for audit logging
- Pagination with configurable page size

---

### 10. **frontend/src/features/patients/components/ConsentCard.tsx** (216 lines)

React component for displaying and revoking patient consent:

**Component Props:**
```typescript
interface ConsentCardProps {
  consent: Consent;
  patientId: string;
}
```

**Features:**

1. **Status Badge:**
   - Color-coded by status: active (green), expired (gray), revoked (red)
   - Displays Consent.status property

2. **Consent Details Display:**
   - Purpose, effective_date, expiration_date (with fallback "No expiration")
   - Granted timestamp, revocation timestamp (if revoked)
   - Physical copy warning badge if on file

3. **Revocation Dialog:**
   - Only shows when consent.status === "active"
   - Modal with backdrop
   - Form fields:
     - Reason for revocation (required, textarea)
     - Physical copy acknowledgment checkbox (if consent.physical_copy_on_file)
   - Disables submit if:
     - Mutation pending
     - Reason empty
     - Physical copy checkbox unchecked (if applicable)

4. **State Management:**
   - `revokeDialogOpen` - Modal visibility
   - `revokeReason` - Textarea value
   - `physicalAcknowledged` - Checkbox state

5. **Mutation:**
   - Calls `revokeConsent(patientId, consent.id, { reason, physical_copy_warning_acknowledged })`
   - On success:
     - Shows toast notification
     - Closes dialog, resets form
     - Invalidates react-query cache for ["consents", patientId]
   - On error:
     - Shows error toast

**UI Elements:**
- Lucide icons: FileText (document), AlertTriangle (warning), X (close), Loader2 (spinner)
- Tailwind CSS with dark mode support
- Responsive grid layout (sm:grid-cols-2)
- Custom button styles with disabled states

---

## Summary

This is a comprehensive medical records management system with:

- **Media Management**: Originality detection, watermarking, copyright infringement tracking
- **Consent Management**: Patient consent lifecycle with revocation support
- **Financial Operations**: Order management, payment processing, daily reconciliation
- **Audit Logging**: Hash-chained, tamper-evident audit trail for compliance
- **Reporting**: Subscription-based report generation and delivery
- **Client Error Tracking**: JavaScript error logging with sensitive field protection

All backend operations include audit context integration for compliance tracking, and the system emphasizes data integrity through hash-chained audit logs and transaction management.

414	    # Transition originality status to "reposted_authorized"
415	    if asset.originality_status == "reposted":
416	        asset.originality_status = "reposted_authorized"
417	        asset.save(update_fields=["originality_status"])
418	
419	    request._audit_context = {
420	        "event_type": "media_repost_authorize",
421	        "target_model": "MediaAsset",
422	        "target_id": str(asset.pk),
423	        "target_repr": asset.original_filename,
424	        "extra_data": {"citation_id": str(citation.pk)},
425	    }
426	
427	    return Response(
428	        CitationSerializer(citation).data,
429	        status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
430	    )
431	
432	
433	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
434	# Attach to Patient
435	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
436	
437	@api_view(["POST"])
438	@permission_classes([IsFrontDeskOrClinicianOrAdmin])
439	def media_attach_patient(request, pk):
440	    """
441	    POST /api/v1/media/{id}/attach-patient/
442	    Link a media asset to a patient.  Accepts ``{ "patient_id": "<uuid>" }``.
443	    """
444	    from apps.mpi.models import Patient
445	
446	    try:
447	        asset = MediaAsset.objects.get(pk=pk, is_deleted=False)
448	    except MediaAsset.DoesNotExist:
449	        return Response(
450	            {
451	                "error": "not_found",
452	                "message": "Media asset not found.",
453	                "status_code": 404,
454	            },
455	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
456	        )
457	
458	    patient_id = request.data.get("patient_id")
459	    if not patient_id:
460	        return Response(
461	            {
462	                "error": "validation_error",
463	                "message": "patient_id is required.",
464	                "status_code": 400,
465	            },
466	            status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
467	        )
468	
469	    # Approval gate: reject disputed media or unauthorized reposts
470	    if asset.originality_status == "disputed":
471	        return Response(
472	            {
473	                "error": "media_not_approved",
474	                "message": "Disputed media cannot be attached to patient materials.",
475	                "status_code": 403,
476	            },
477	            status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
478	        )
479	    if asset.originality_status == "reposted":
480	        from .models import Citation
481	        has_auth = Citation.objects.filter(
482	            media_asset=asset,
483	        ).exclude(citation_text="").exclude(authorization_file_path="").exists()
484	        if not has_auth:
485	            return Response(
486	                {
487	                    "error": "media_not_approved",
488	                    "message": "Reposted media requires citation and authorization before attaching.",
489	                    "status_code": 403,
490	                },
491	                status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
492	            )
493	
494	    try:
495	        patient = Patient.objects.get(pk=patient_id, is_active=True)
496	    except Patient.DoesNotExist:
497	        return Response(
498	            {
499	                "error": "not_found",
500	                "message": "Patient not found or inactive.",
501	                "status_code": 404,
502	            },
503	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
504	        )
505	
506	    asset.patient = patient
507	    asset.save(update_fields=["patient", "updated_at"])
508	
509	    request._audit_context = {
510	        "event_type": "media_attach_patient",
511	        "target_model": "MediaAsset",
512	        "target_id": str(asset.pk),
513	        "target_repr": asset.original_filename,
514	        "extra_data": {"patient_id": str(patient.pk)},
515	    }
516	
517	    return Response(MediaDetailSerializer(asset).data)
518	
519	
520	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
521	# Infringement CRUD
522	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
523	
524	@api_view(["GET", "POST"])
525	@permission_classes([IsComplianceOrAdmin])
526	def infringement_list_create(request):
527	    """
528	    GET  /api/v1/media/infringement/  -- list infringement reports
529	    POST /api/v1/media/infringement/  -- create an infringement report
530	    Compliance / admin only.
531	    """
532	    # -- POST: create -------------------------------------------------------
533	    if request.method == "POST":
534	        serializer = InfringementCreateSerializer(
535	            data=request.data,
536	            context={"request": request},
537	        )
538	        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)
539	        report = serializer.save()
540	
541	        # Transition linked media to disputed status
542	        if report.media_asset and report.media_asset.originality_status != "disputed":
543	            report.media_asset.originality_status = "disputed"
544	            report.media_asset.save(update_fields=["originality_status"])
545	
546	        request._audit_context = {
547	            "event_type": "media_infringement_create",
548	            "target_model": "InfringementReport",
549	            "target_id": str(report.pk),
550	            "target_repr": f"Infringement #{report.pk}",
551	        }
552	
553	        return Response(
554	            InfringementDetailSerializer(report).data,
555	            status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
556	        )
557	
558	    # -- GET: list ----------------------------------------------------------
559	    qs = InfringementReport.objects.select_related("media_asset").all()
560	
561	    status_filter = request.query_params.get("status")
562	    if status_filter:
563	        qs = qs.filter(status=status_filter)
564	
565	    page = int(request.query_params.get("page", 1))
566	    page_size = int(request.query_params.get("page_size", 20))
567	    start = (page - 1) * page_size
568	    end = start + page_size
569	
570	    total = qs.count()
571	    reports = qs[start:end]
572	
573	    return Response({
574	        "count": total,
575	        "page": page,
576	        "page_size": page_size,
577	        "results": InfringementListSerializer(reports, many=True).data,
578	    })
579	
580	
581	@api_view(["GET", "PATCH"])
582	@permission_classes([IsComplianceOrAdmin])
583	def infringement_detail_update(request, pk):
584	    """
585	    GET   /api/v1/media/infringement/{id}/  -- detail
586	    PATCH /api/v1/media/infringement/{id}/  -- update status
587	    Compliance / admin only.
588	    """
589	    try:
590	        report = InfringementReport.objects.select_related(
591	            "media_asset", "reporter", "assigned_to"
592	        ).get(pk=pk)
593	    except InfringementReport.DoesNotExist:
594	        return Response(
595	            {
596	                "error": "not_found",
597	                "message": "Infringement report not found.",
598	                "status_code": 404,
599	            },
600	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
601	        )
602	
603	    # -- GET: detail --------------------------------------------------------
604	    if request.method == "GET":
605	        return Response(InfringementDetailSerializer(report).data)
606	
607	    # -- PATCH: update status -----------------------------------------------
608	    serializer = InfringementUpdateSerializer(
609	        data=request.data,
610	        context={"request": request, "report": report},
611	    )
612	    serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)
613	
614	    old_status = report.status
615	    new_status = serializer.validated_data["status"]
616	    report = serializer.update(report, serializer.validated_data)
617	
618	    # On resolution/dismissal, revert media originality if no other open reports
619	    if new_status in ("resolved", "dismissed") and report.media_asset:
620	        other_open = InfringementReport.objects.filter(
621	            media_asset=report.media_asset,
622	            status__in=["open", "investigating"],
623	        ).exclude(pk=report.pk).exists()
624	        if not other_open:
625	            # Revert: check if authorized citation exists
626	            has_authorized_citation = Citation.objects.filter(
627	                media_asset=report.media_asset,
628	            ).exclude(citation_text="").exclude(authorization_file_path="").exists()
629	            has_any_citation = Citation.objects.filter(media_asset=report.media_asset).exists()
630	            if has_authorized_citation:
631	                report.media_asset.originality_status = "reposted_authorized"
632	            elif has_any_citation:
633	                report.media_asset.originality_status = "reposted"
634	            else:
635	                report.media_asset.originality_status = "original"
636	            report.media_asset.save(update_fields=["originality_status"])
637	
638	    request._audit_context = {
639	        "event_type": "media_infringement_update",
640	        "target_model": "InfringementReport",
641	        "target_id": str(report.pk),
642	        "target_repr": f"Infringement #{report.pk}",
643	        "field_changes": {
644	            "status": {"old": old_status, "new": report.status},
645	        },
646	    }
647	
648	    return Response(InfringementDetailSerializer(report).data)
649

1	"""Consent models: Consent and ConsentScope."""
2	import uuid
3	from datetime import date
4	
5	from django.conf import settings
6	from django.db import models
7	
8	from domain.services.consent_service import compute_consent_status
9	
10	
11	class Consent(models.Model):
12	    """
13	    Patient consent record tracking authorisation for data use,
14	    treatment, media use, or third-party sharing.
15	    """
16	
17	    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
18	    patient = models.ForeignKey(
19	        "mpi.Patient",
20	        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
21	        related_name="consents",
22	    )
23	    purpose = models.CharField(max_length=200)
24	
25	    # Who granted it
26	    granted_by = models.ForeignKey(
27	        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
28	        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
29	        related_name="granted_consents",
30	    )
31	    granted_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
32	
33	    # Date range
34	    effective_date = models.DateField()
35	    expiration_date = models.DateField(
36	        null=True,
37	        blank=True,
38	        help_text="Null means the consent is indefinite.",
39	    )
40	
41	    # Revocation
42	    is_revoked = models.BooleanField(default=False)
43	    revoked_at = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
44	    revoked_by = models.ForeignKey(
45	        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
46	        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
47	        null=True,
48	        blank=True,
49	        related_name="revoked_consents",
50	    )
51	    revocation_reason = models.TextField(blank=True, default="")
52	
53	    # Physical copy tracking
54	    physical_copy_on_file = models.BooleanField(default=False)
55	    physical_copy_warning_shown = models.BooleanField(default=False)
56	
57	    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
58	    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
59	
60	    class Meta:
61	        db_table = "consents"
62	        ordering = ["-granted_at"]
63	
64	    def __str__(self) -> str:
65	        return f"Consent {self.id} ({self.purpose}) for patient {self.patient_id}"
66	
67	    @property
68	    def status(self) -> str:
69	        """Computed status: 'active', 'expired', or 'revoked'."""
70	        return compute_consent_status(
71	            is_revoked=self.is_revoked,
72	            revoked_at=self.revoked_at,
73	            expiration_date=self.expiration_date,
74	            effective_date=self.effective_date,
75	        )
76	
77	
78	class ConsentScope(models.Model):
79	    """
80	    Fine-grained scope entries attached to a consent -- e.g. which
81	    data fields, actions, media uses, or third parties are covered.
82	    """
83	
84	    SCOPE_TYPE_CHOICES = [
85	        ("data_field", "Data Field"),
86	        ("action", "Action"),
87	        ("media_use", "Media Use"),
88	        ("third_party", "Third Party"),
89	    ]
90	
91	    id = models.BigAutoField(primary_key=True)
92	    consent = models.ForeignKey(
93	        Consent,
94	        on_delete=models.CASCADE,
95	        related_name="scopes",
96	    )
97	    scope_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=SCOPE_TYPE_CHOICES)
98	    scope_value = models.CharField(max_length=200)
99	
100	    class Meta:
101	        db_table = "consent_scopes"
102	
103	    def __str__(self) -> str:
104	        return f"{self.scope_type}:{self.scope_value}"
105

1	"""DRF serializers for the Media & Originality Engine."""
2	import logging
3	import os
4	import uuid
5	from datetime import date
6	
7	from django.conf import settings
8	from django.db import transaction
9	from django.utils import timezone
10	from rest_framework import serializers
11	
12	from apps.media_engine.models import (
13	    Citation,
14	    DisputeHistory,
15	    InfringementReport,
16	    MediaAsset,
17	    MediaDerivative,
18	)
19	from apps.media_engine.services import (
20	    check_originality,
21	    compute_file_hash,
22	    compute_pixel_hash,
23	    extract_evidence_metadata,
24	    validate_file_type,
25	)
26	from domain.services.media_service import validate_infringement_transition
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger("medrights.media")
29	
30	MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE = 20 * 1024 * 1024  # 20 MB
31	
32	
33	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	# Upload
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	class MediaUploadSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
38	    """
39	    Accepts a file upload, fingerprints it, checks originality, and
40	    creates a MediaAsset record.
41	    """
42	
43	    file = serializers.FileField(required=True)
44	    patient_id = serializers.UUIDField(required=False, allow_null=True)
45	    consent_id = serializers.UUIDField(required=False, allow_null=True)
46	    watermark_config = serializers.JSONField(required=False, allow_null=True)
47	
48	    def validate_file(self, value):
49	        """Check magic-byte type and size."""
50	        if value.size > MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE:
51	            raise serializers.ValidationError(
52	                f"File size {value.size} bytes exceeds the 20 MB limit."
53	            )
54	        try:
55	            detected_mime = validate_file_type(value)
56	        except ValueError as exc:
57	            raise serializers.ValidationError(str(exc))
58	
59	        # Stash the detected MIME for later use in create()
60	        value._detected_mime = detected_mime
61	        return value
62	
63	    @transaction.atomic
64	    def create(self, validated_data):
65	        file_obj = validated_data["file"]
66	        user = self.context["request"].user
67	        detected_mime = getattr(file_obj, "_detected_mime", "application/octet-stream")
68	
69	        # Compute hashes
70	        try:
71	            pixel_hash = compute_pixel_hash(file_obj)
72	        except ValueError as exc:
73	            raise serializers.ValidationError({"file": str(exc)})
74	        file_hash = compute_file_hash(file_obj)
75	
76	        # Originality check
77	        originality_status, matching_asset = check_originality(pixel_hash)
78	
79	        # Extract evidence metadata (immutable, set once)
80	        evidence_metadata = extract_evidence_metadata(file_obj)
81	
82	        # Build date-based storage path: storage/media/YYYY/MM/
83	        today = date.today()
84	        relative_dir = os.path.join("media", str(today.year), f"{today.month:02d}")
85	        absolute_dir = os.path.join(settings.MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT, relative_dir)
86	        os.makedirs(absolute_dir, exist_ok=True)
87	
88	        # Unique filename
89	        ext = os.path.splitext(file_obj.name)[1] or ".bin"
90	        stored_name = f"{uuid.uuid4().hex}{ext}"
91	        relative_path = os.path.join(relative_dir, stored_name)
92	        absolute_path = os.path.join(settings.MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT, relative_path)
93	
94	        # Write file to disk
95	        file_obj.seek(0)
96	        with open(absolute_path, "wb") as dest:
97	            for chunk in file_obj.chunks():
98	                dest.write(chunk)
99	
100	        # Resolve optional FKs
101	        patient = None
102	        consent = None
103	        patient_id = validated_data.get("patient_id")
104	        consent_id = validated_data.get("consent_id")
105	
106	        if patient_id:
107	            from apps.mpi.models import Patient
108	            try:
109	                patient = Patient.objects.get(pk=patient_id)
110	            except Patient.DoesNotExist:
111	                raise serializers.ValidationError(
112	                    {"patient_id": "Patient not found."}
113	                )
114	
115	        if consent_id:
116	            from apps.consent.models import Consent
117	            try:
118	                consent = Consent.objects.get(pk=consent_id)
119	            except Consent.DoesNotExist:
120	                raise serializers.ValidationError(
121	                    {"consent_id": "Consent record not found."}
122	                )
123	
124	        asset = MediaAsset.objects.create(
125	            patient=patient,
126	            consent=consent,
127	            original_file=relative_path,
128	            original_filename=file_obj.name,
129	            mime_type=detected_mime,
130	            file_size_bytes=file_obj.size,
131	            pixel_hash=pixel_hash,
132	            file_hash=file_hash,
133	            originality_status=originality_status,
134	            watermark_settings=validated_data.get("watermark_config"),
135	            evidence_metadata=evidence_metadata,
136	            uploaded_by=user,
137	        )
138	
139	        return asset
140	
141	
142	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
143	# Detail / List
144	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145	
146	class MediaDetailSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
147	    """Full detail view of a MediaAsset."""
148	
149	    patient_id = serializers.UUIDField(source="patient.pk", allow_null=True, read_only=True)
150	    consent_id = serializers.UUIDField(source="consent.pk", allow_null=True, read_only=True)
151	    uploaded_by_id = serializers.UUIDField(source="uploaded_by.pk", read_only=True)
152	    repost_authorized = serializers.SerializerMethodField()
153	
154	    class Meta:
155	        model = MediaAsset
156	        fields = [
157	            "id",
158	            "patient_id",
159	            "consent_id",
160	            "original_file",
161	            "watermarked_file",
162	            "original_filename",
163	            "mime_type",
164	            "file_size_bytes",
165	            "pixel_hash",
166	            "file_hash",
167	            "originality_status",
168	            "watermark_settings",
169	            "watermark_burned",
170	            "evidence_metadata",
171	            "uploaded_by_id",
172	            "is_deleted",
173	            "created_at",
174	            "updated_at",
175	            "repost_authorized",
176	        ]
177	        read_only_fields = fields
178	
179	    def get_repost_authorized(self, obj):
180	        """Return whether reposted media has a valid citation + authorization.
181	
182	        Returns True for ``reposted_authorized`` status (already approved),
183	        False for ``reposted`` without citation, and None for non-repost states.
184	        """
185	        if obj.originality_status == "reposted_authorized":
186	            return True
187	        if obj.originality_status == "reposted":
188	            return Citation.objects.filter(
189	                media_asset=obj,
190	                citation_text__isnull=False,
191	                authorization_file_path__isnull=False,
192	            ).exclude(citation_text="").exclude(authorization_file_path="").exists()
193	        return None
194	
195	
196	class MediaListSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
197	    """Lightweight list representation."""
198	
199	    patient_id = serializers.UUIDField(source="patient.pk", allow_null=True, read_only=True)
200	
201	    class Meta:
202	        model = MediaAsset
203	        fields = [
204	            "id",
205	            "original_filename",
206	            "originality_status",
207	            "patient_id",
208	            "created_at",
209	            "mime_type",
210	            "file_size_bytes",
211	            "watermark_burned",
212	        ]
213	        read_only_fields = fields
214	
215	
216	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	# Watermark
218	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
219	
220	class WatermarkSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
221	    """Input for server-side watermark burn."""
222	
223	    clinic_name = serializers.CharField(max_length=200)
224	    date_stamp = serializers.BooleanField(default=True)
225	    opacity = serializers.FloatField(min_value=0.0, max_value=1.0, default=0.35)
226	
227	
228	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
229	# Repost / Citation
230	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
231	
232	class RepostAuthorizeSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
233	    """Attach a citation + authorisation document to a media asset."""
234	
235	    citation_text = serializers.CharField(min_length=20)
236	    authorization_file = serializers.FileField(required=True)
237	
238	
239	class CitationSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
240	    """Read-only citation representation."""
241	
242	    class Meta:
243	        model = Citation
244	        fields = [
245	            "id",
246	            "media_asset_id",
247	            "citation_text",
248	            "authorization_file_path",
249	            "approved_by_id",
