255	
256	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
257	# Infringement
258	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	
260	class InfringementCreateSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
261	    """Create an infringement report. Only compliance / admin roles."""
262	
263	    media_asset_id = serializers.UUIDField(required=False, allow_null=True)
264	    screenshot = serializers.FileField(required=False, allow_null=True)
265	    reference = serializers.CharField(max_length=2048, required=False, allow_null=True)
266	    notes = serializers.CharField()
267	
268	    def validate(self, data):
269	        screenshot = data.get("screenshot")
270	        reference = data.get("reference")
271	        if not screenshot and not reference:
272	            raise serializers.ValidationError(
273	                "At least one of 'screenshot' or 'reference' must be provided."
274	            )
275	        return data
276	
277	    @transaction.atomic
278	    def create(self, validated_data):
279	        user = self.context["request"].user
280	
281	        media_asset = None
282	        asset_id = validated_data.get("media_asset_id")
283	        if asset_id:
284	            try:
285	                media_asset = MediaAsset.objects.get(pk=asset_id, is_deleted=False)
286	            except MediaAsset.DoesNotExist:
287	                raise serializers.ValidationError(
288	                    {"media_asset_id": "Media asset not found."}
289	                )
290	
291	        # Save optional screenshot
292	        screenshot_path = None
293	        screenshot_file = validated_data.get("screenshot")
294	        if screenshot_file:
295	            today = date.today()
296	            rel_dir = os.path.join(
297	                "infringements", str(today.year), f"{today.month:02d}"
298	            )
299	            abs_dir = os.path.join(settings.MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT, rel_dir)
300	            os.makedirs(abs_dir, exist_ok=True)
301	            ext = os.path.splitext(screenshot_file.name)[1] or ".bin"
302	            fname = f"{uuid.uuid4().hex}{ext}"
303	            screenshot_path = os.path.join(rel_dir, fname)
304	            abs_path = os.path.join(settings.MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT, screenshot_path)
305	            with open(abs_path, "wb") as f:
306	                for chunk in screenshot_file.chunks():
307	                    f.write(chunk)
308	
309	        report = InfringementReport.objects.create(
310	            media_asset=media_asset,
311	            reporter=user,
312	            screenshot_path=screenshot_path,
313	            reference=validated_data.get("reference"),
314	            notes=validated_data["notes"],
315	        )
316	        return report
317	
318	
319	class InfringementDetailSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
320	    """Read-only detail of an infringement report."""
321	
322	    reporter_id = serializers.UUIDField(source="reporter.pk", read_only=True)
323	    media_asset_id = serializers.UUIDField(
324	        source="media_asset.pk", allow_null=True, read_only=True
325	    )
326	    assigned_to_id = serializers.UUIDField(
327	        source="assigned_to.pk", allow_null=True, read_only=True
328	    )
329	
330	    class Meta:
331	        model = InfringementReport
332	        fields = [
333	            "id",
334	            "media_asset_id",
335	            "reporter_id",
336	            "screenshot_path",
337	            "reference",
338	            "notes",
339	            "status",
340	            "resolution_notes",
341	            "assigned_to_id",
342	            "opened_at",
343	            "investigating_at",
344	            "resolved_at",
345	            "dismissed_at",
346	            "created_at",
347	            "updated_at",
348	        ]
349	        read_only_fields = fields
350	
351	
352	class InfringementListSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
353	    """Lightweight list representation for infringement reports."""
354	
355	    media_asset_id = serializers.UUIDField(
356	        source="media_asset.pk", allow_null=True, read_only=True
357	    )
358	
359	    class Meta:
360	        model = InfringementReport
361	        fields = [
362	            "id",
363	            "media_asset_id",
364	            "status",
365	            "notes",
366	            "opened_at",
367	            "created_at",
368	        ]
369	        read_only_fields = fields
370	
371	
372	class InfringementUpdateSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
373	    """
374	    Update the status of an infringement report. Validates allowed
375	    state transitions and creates a DisputeHistory record.
376	    """
377	
378	    status = serializers.ChoiceField(
379	        choices=[c[0] for c in InfringementReport.STATUS_CHOICES],
380	    )
381	    notes = serializers.CharField(required=False, default="")
382	
383	    def validate(self, attrs):
384	        report = self.context["report"]
385	        new_status = attrs["status"]
386	
387	        if not validate_infringement_transition(report.status, new_status):
388	            raise serializers.ValidationError(
389	                {
390	                    "status": (
391	                        f"Invalid transition from '{report.status}' to "
392	                        f"'{new_status}'. Allowed targets: "
393	                        f"{', '.join(sorted(self._allowed_targets(report.status))) or 'none'}."
394	                    )
395	                }
396	            )
397	        return attrs
398	
399	    @staticmethod
400	    def _allowed_targets(current_status: str):
401	        from domain.services.media_service import VALID_TRANSITIONS
402	        return VALID_TRANSITIONS.get(current_status, set())
403	
404	    @transaction.atomic
405	    def update(self, report, validated_data):
406	        user = self.context["request"].user
407	        old_status = report.status
408	        new_status = validated_data["status"]
409	        notes = validated_data.get("notes", "")
410	
411	        # Update report fields
412	        report.status = new_status
413	        report.resolution_notes = notes
414	
415	        now = timezone.now()
416	        if new_status == "investigating":
417	            report.investigating_at = now
418	        elif new_status == "resolved":
419	            report.resolved_at = now
420	        elif new_status == "dismissed":
421	            report.dismissed_at = now
422	
423	        report.save()
424	
425	        # Create dispute history record
426	        DisputeHistory.objects.create(
427	            report=report,
428	            old_status=old_status,
429	            new_status=new_status,
430	            changed_by=user,
431	            notes=notes,
432	        )
433	
434	        return report
435

1	"""Celery tasks for the financials app."""
2	import csv
3	import logging
4	import os
5	from datetime import date, timedelta
6	from decimal import Decimal
7	
8	from celery import shared_task
9	from django.conf import settings
10	from django.db import transaction
11	from django.db.models import Sum
12	from django.utils import timezone
13	
14	logger = logging.getLogger("medrights.financials")
15	
16	
17	@shared_task(name="apps.financials.tasks.auto_close_unpaid_orders")
18	def auto_close_unpaid_orders():
19	    """
20	    Close orders that have been open past their auto_close_at deadline.
21	
22	    Runs every 60 seconds via Celery Beat. Idempotent -- only touches
23	    orders that are still 'open' and have passed their deadline.
24	    """
25	    from apps.financials.models import Order
26	
27	    now = timezone.now()
28	
29	    # Only select orders that are open and past their auto-close deadline
30	    expired_orders = Order.objects.filter(
31	        status="open",
32	        auto_close_at__isnull=False,
33	        auto_close_at__lte=now,
34	    )
35	
36	    closed_count = 0
37	    for order in expired_orders.iterator():
38	        order.status = "closed_unpaid"
39	        order.save(update_fields=["status", "updated_at"])
40	        closed_count += 1
41	        logger.info(
42	            "Auto-closed unpaid order: order_number=%s auto_close_at=%s",
43	            order.order_number,
44	            order.auto_close_at,
45	        )
46	
47	    if closed_count:
48	        logger.info("Auto-close task completed: %d order(s) closed.", closed_count)
49	
50	    return {"closed_count": closed_count}
51	
52	
53	@shared_task(name="apps.financials.tasks.generate_daily_reconciliation")
54	def generate_daily_reconciliation(target_date=None):
55	    """
56	    Generate an end-of-day reconciliation report for a given date.
57	
58	    If target_date is None, uses yesterday. Idempotent -- will not
59	    regenerate if a record already exists for that date.
60	
61	    Args:
62	        target_date: ISO-format date string (YYYY-MM-DD) or None.
63	    """
64	    from apps.financials.models import (
65	        DailyReconciliation,
66	        Order,
67	        Payment,
68	        Refund,
69	    )
70	
71	    if target_date:
72	        if isinstance(target_date, str):
73	            target = date.fromisoformat(target_date)
74	        else:
75	            target = target_date
76	    else:
77	        target = date.today() - timedelta(days=1)
78	
79	    # Idempotency check
80	    if DailyReconciliation.objects.filter(reconciliation_date=target).exists():
81	        logger.info(
82	            "Reconciliation already exists for %s, skipping.", target
83	        )
84	        return {"status": "already_exists", "date": str(target)}
85	
86	    # Aggregate data for the target date
87	    orders_qs = Order.objects.filter(created_at__date=target)
88	    total_orders = orders_qs.count()
89	    total_revenue = orders_qs.aggregate(
90	        total=Sum("total_amount")
91	    )["total"] or Decimal("0.00")
92	
93	    payments_qs = Payment.objects.filter(posted_at__date=target)
94	    total_payments = payments_qs.aggregate(
95	        total=Sum("amount")
96	    )["total"] or Decimal("0.00")
97	
98	    refunds_qs = Refund.objects.filter(
99	        completed_at__date=target,
100	        status="completed",
101	    )
102	    total_refunds = refunds_qs.aggregate(
103	        total=Sum("amount")
104	    )["total"] or Decimal("0.00")
105	
106	    discrepancy = total_payments - total_refunds - total_revenue
107	
108	    # Generate CSV
109	    storage_root = getattr(settings, "MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT", "storage")
110	    reconciliation_dir = os.path.join(storage_root, "reconciliation")
111	    os.makedirs(reconciliation_dir, exist_ok=True)
112	
113	    csv_filename = f"reconciliation_{target}.csv"
114	    csv_path = os.path.join(reconciliation_dir, csv_filename)
115	
116	    with open(csv_path, "w", newline="") as csvfile:
117	        writer = csv.writer(csvfile)
118	        writer.writerow([
119	            "Date", "Total Orders", "Total Revenue",
120	            "Total Payments", "Total Refunds", "Discrepancy",
121	        ])
122	        writer.writerow([
123	            str(target),
124	            total_orders,
125	            str(total_revenue),
126	            str(total_payments),
127	            str(total_refunds),
128	            str(discrepancy),
129	        ])
130	
131	        # Detail: orders
132	        writer.writerow([])
133	        writer.writerow(["Order Number", "Status", "Total", "Paid", "Patient ID"])
134	        for order in orders_qs.iterator():
135	            writer.writerow([
136	                order.order_number,
137	                order.status,
138	                str(order.total_amount),
139	                str(order.amount_paid),
140	                str(order.patient_id),
141	            ])
142	
143	        # Detail: payments
144	        writer.writerow([])
145	        writer.writerow(["Payment ID", "Order Number", "Amount", "Method", "Posted At"])
146	        for payment in payments_qs.select_related("order").iterator():
147	            writer.writerow([
148	                str(payment.id),
149	                payment.order.order_number,
150	                str(payment.amount),
151	                payment.payment_method,
152	                payment.posted_at.isoformat() if payment.posted_at else "",
153	            ])
154	
155	    # Determine if this is a deferred reconciliation (not run by celery_beat same day)
156	    is_deferred = target < date.today() - timedelta(days=1)
157	
158	    # Create reconciliation record
159	    record = DailyReconciliation.objects.create(
160	        reconciliation_date=target,
161	        total_orders=total_orders,
162	        total_revenue=total_revenue,
163	        total_payments=total_payments,
164	        total_refunds=total_refunds,
165	        discrepancy=discrepancy,
166	        csv_file_path=csv_path,
167	        generated_by="celery_beat",
168	        is_deferred=is_deferred,
169	    )
170	
171	    logger.info(
172	        "Reconciliation generated: date=%s orders=%d revenue=%s payments=%s refunds=%s discrepancy=%s",
173	        target,
174	        total_orders,
175	        total_revenue,
176	        total_payments,
177	        total_refunds,
178	        discrepancy,
179	    )
180	
181	    return {
182	        "status": "generated",
183	        "date": str(target),
184	        "record_id": str(record.id),
185	    }
186	
187	
188	@shared_task(name="apps.financials.tasks.check_deferred_reconciliation")
189	def check_deferred_reconciliation():
190	    """
191	    Check the last 7 days for missing reconciliation records and generate
192	    each one.  Called on user login to catch up after offline periods.
193	    """
194	    from apps.financials.models import DailyReconciliation
195	
196	    today = date.today()
197	    generated = []
198	
199	    for days_ago in range(1, 8):
200	        target = today - timedelta(days=days_ago)
201	
202	        if not DailyReconciliation.objects.filter(reconciliation_date=target).exists():
203	            logger.info(
204	                "Missing reconciliation for %s, generating deferred report.",
205	                target,
206	            )
207	            result = generate_daily_reconciliation(str(target))
208	            generated.append(str(target))
209	
210	    if generated:
211	        logger.info(
212	            "Deferred reconciliation complete: generated %d report(s) for %s",
213	            len(generated),
214	            ", ".join(generated),
215	        )
216	
217	    return {"generated_dates": generated, "count": len(generated)}
218

1	"""Audit logging middleware: captures all auditable requests.
2	
3	Writes an audit entry for ANY HTTP method (including GET) when the
4	view has populated ``request._audit_context``.  This ensures
5	read-access events (patient views, consent lookups, break-glass
6	reads, search queries) are logged alongside state-changing operations.
7	"""
8	import logging
9	
10	logger = logging.getLogger("medrights.audit")
11	
12	
13	class AuditLoggingMiddleware:
14	    """
15	    After response processing, checks if the view annotated
16	    request._audit_context and creates an audit log entry.
17	    Works for all HTTP methods -- views opt in by setting the context.
18	    """
19	
20	    def __init__(self, get_response):
21	        self.get_response = get_response
22	
23	    def __call__(self, request):
24	        request._audit_context = {}
25	
26	        response = self.get_response(request)
27	
28	        if (
29	            hasattr(request, "_audit_context")
30	            and request._audit_context.get("event_type")
31	        ):
32	            try:
33	                from apps.audit.service import create_audit_entry
34	
35	                ctx = request._audit_context
36	                create_audit_entry(
37	                    event_type=ctx["event_type"],
38	                    user=request.user if request.user.is_authenticated else None,
39	                    username_snapshot=getattr(request.user, "username", "anonymous"),
40	                    client_ip=getattr(request, "client_ip", ""),
41	                    workstation_id=getattr(request, "workstation_id", ""),
42	                    target_model=ctx.get("target_model", ""),
43	                    target_id=str(ctx.get("target_id", "")),
44	                    target_repr=ctx.get("target_repr", ""),
45	                    field_changes=ctx.get("field_changes", {}),
46	                    extra_data=ctx.get("extra_data", {}),
47	                )
48	            except Exception:
49	                logger.exception("Failed to create audit entry")
50	
51	        return response
52

1	"""
2	Audit logging service.
3	
4	Provides hash-chained, tamper-evident audit logging.  Each new entry's
5	SHA-256 hash is computed from its canonical JSON payload concatenated
6	with the previous entry's hash, forming a verifiable chain.
7	"""
8	import hashlib
9	import json
10	import logging
11	
12	from django.db import transaction
13	
14	logger = logging.getLogger("medrights.audit")
15	
16	
17	def _canonical_json(data: dict) -> str:
18	    """Return a deterministic JSON string for hashing."""
19	    return json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True, default=str)
20	
21	
22	def _compute_hash(entry_data: dict, previous_hash: str) -> str:
23	    """SHA-256( canonical_json(entry_data) + previous_hash )."""
24	    payload = _canonical_json(entry_data) + previous_hash
25	    return hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
26	
27	
28	def create_audit_entry(
29	    event_type: str,
30	    user=None,
31	    username_snapshot: str = "",
32	    client_ip: str = "",
33	    workstation_id: str = "",
34	    target_model: str = "",
35	    target_id: str = "",
36	    target_repr: str = "",
37	    field_changes: dict | None = None,
38	    extra_data: dict | None = None,
39	):
40	    """
41	    Atomically create a hash-chained audit entry.
42	
43	    Parameters
44	    ----------
45	    event_type : str
46	        The event type (should match an EventType choice).
47	    user : User | None
48	        The Django user who performed the action.
49	    username_snapshot : str
50	        Username at the time of the event (survives user renames/deletes).
51	    client_ip : str
52	        IP address of the client.
53	    workstation_id : str
54	        Workstation identifier from X-Workstation-ID header.
55	    target_model : str
56	        The model name of the affected entity.
57	    target_id : str
58	        The primary key of the affected entity.
59	    target_repr : str
60	        A human-readable representation of the target.
61	    field_changes : dict | None
62	        Dict of changed fields: {"field": {"old": ..., "new": ...}}.
63	    extra_data : dict | None
64	        Any additional structured data.
65	
66	    Returns
67	    -------
68	    AuditEntry
69	        The newly created audit entry.
70	    """
71	    # Normalize dotted event types to underscore format
72	    event_type = event_type.replace(".", "_")
73	
74	    # Lazy import to avoid circular imports at module load time.
75	    from .models import GENESIS_HASH, AuditEntry
76	
77	    field_changes = field_changes or {}
78	    extra_data = extra_data or {}
79	
80	    # Normalise client_ip: empty strings become None for GenericIPAddressField.
81	    ip_value = client_ip if client_ip else None
82	
83	    # Build the data dict used for hashing (excluding computed fields).
84	    entry_data = {
85	        "event_type": event_type,
86	        "user_id": str(user.pk) if user else None,
87	        "username_snapshot": username_snapshot,
88	        "client_ip": client_ip,
89	        "workstation_id": workstation_id,
90	        "target_model": target_model,
91	        "target_id": target_id,
92	        "target_repr": target_repr,
93	        "field_changes": field_changes,
94	        "extra_data": extra_data,
95	    }
96	
97	    try:
98	        with transaction.atomic():
99	            # Lock the last row to prevent concurrent writers from
100	            # creating entries with the same previous_hash.
101	            last_entry = (
102	                AuditEntry.objects
103	                .select_for_update()
104	                .order_by("-id")
105	                .first()
106	            )
107	
108	            previous_hash = last_entry.entry_hash if last_entry else GENESIS_HASH
109	            entry_hash = _compute_hash(entry_data, previous_hash)
110	
111	            audit_entry = AuditEntry.objects.create(
112	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
113	                previous_hash=previous_hash,
114	                event_type=event_type,
115	                user=user,
116	                username_snapshot=username_snapshot,
117	                client_ip=ip_value,
118	                workstation_id=workstation_id,
119	                target_model=target_model,
120	                target_id=target_id,
121	                target_repr=target_repr,
122	                field_changes=field_changes,
123	                extra_data=extra_data,
124	            )
125	
126	        logger.info(
127	            "AUDIT: %s | %s | %s",
128	            event_type,
129	            username_snapshot or "anonymous",
130	            target_repr,
131	            extra={"audit_entry": entry_data, "entry_id": audit_entry.pk},
132	        )
133	
134	        return audit_entry
135	
136	    except Exception:
137	        # Always log even if the DB write fails, so the event is not lost.
138	        logger.exception(
139	            "AUDIT WRITE FAILED: %s | %s | %s",
140	            event_type,
141	            username_snapshot or "anonymous",
142	            target_repr,
143	            extra={"audit_entry": entry_data},
144	        )
145	        raise
146	
147	
148	def verify_audit_chain() -> tuple[bool, int | None, int]:
149	    """
150	    Walk the entire audit chain and verify every entry's hash.
151	
152	    Handles gaps from archived (purged) segments by consulting
153	    AuditArchiveSegment records.  If the first remaining entry's
154	    previous_hash matches a known archive segment boundary hash,
155	    the gap is bridged and verification continues.
156	
157	    Returns
158	    -------
159	    tuple of (is_valid, broken_at_id, total_checked)
160	        is_valid : bool
161	            True if the entire chain is intact.
162	        broken_at_id : int | None
163	            The ID of the first entry whose hash does not match, or
164	            None if the chain is valid.
165	        total_checked : int
166	            The number of entries inspected.
167	    """
168	    from .models import GENESIS_HASH, AuditArchiveSegment, AuditEntry
169	
170	    # Build a set of known archive boundary hashes for quick lookup
171	    archive_boundary_hashes = set(
172	        AuditArchiveSegment.objects.values_list("segment_end_hash", flat=True)
173	    )
174	
175	    entries = AuditEntry.objects.order_by("id").iterator(chunk_size=1000)
176	    expected_previous_hash = GENESIS_HASH
177	    total_checked = 0
178	
179	    for entry in entries:
180	        total_checked += 1
181	
182	        # Verify previous_hash linkage.
183	        if entry.previous_hash != expected_previous_hash:
184	            # Check if the gap is due to an archived segment
185	            if entry.previous_hash in archive_boundary_hashes:
186	                # The entry's previous_hash matches a known archive
187	                # segment boundary, so the chain bridges correctly.
188	                pass
189	            elif total_checked == 1 and expected_previous_hash == GENESIS_HASH:
190	                # First remaining entry after a purge -- check if its
191	                # previous_hash is a known archive boundary.
192	                if entry.previous_hash not in archive_boundary_hashes:
193	                    return False, entry.pk, total_checked
194	            else:
195	                return False, entry.pk, total_checked
196	
197	        # Reconstruct the data dict that was hashed at creation time.
198	        entry_data = {
199	            "event_type": entry.event_type,
200	            "user_id": str(entry.user_id) if entry.user_id else None,
201	            "username_snapshot": entry.username_snapshot,
202	            "client_ip": str(entry.client_ip) if entry.client_ip else "",
203	            "workstation_id": entry.workstation_id,
204	            "target_model": entry.target_model,
205	            "target_id": entry.target_id,
206	            "target_repr": entry.target_repr,
207	            "field_changes": entry.field_changes,
208	            "extra_data": entry.extra_data,
209	        }
210	
211	        recomputed_hash = _compute_hash(entry_data, entry.previous_hash)
212	        if recomputed_hash != entry.entry_hash:
213	            return False, entry.pk, total_checked
214	
215	        expected_previous_hash = entry.entry_hash
216	
217	    return True, None, total_checked
218

1	"""Client-side error logging endpoint for JavaScript error reports."""
2	import logging
3	
4	from rest_framework import status
5	from rest_framework.decorators import api_view, permission_classes, throttle_classes
6	from rest_framework.permissions import AllowAny
7	from rest_framework.response import Response
8	from rest_framework.throttling import AnonRateThrottle
9	
10	logger = logging.getLogger("medrights.audit")
11	
12	# Maximum size of the message and stack fields to prevent abuse
13	MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH = 2000
14	MAX_STACK_LENGTH = 5000
15	MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 10 * 1024  # 10 KB
16	
17	# Only these top-level fields are accepted in the payload
18	ALLOWED_FIELDS = {"message", "stack", "url", "timestamp", "component", "level", "user_agent", "extra"}
19	
20	# Any field whose name contains one of these words (case-insensitive) is
21	# considered sensitive and must be rejected or stripped.
22	SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS = {"password", "token", "secret", "key", "ssn", "dob", "mrn"}
23	
24	
25	class ClientErrorRateThrottle(AnonRateThrottle):
26	    """Scoped throttle: 10 requests per minute for anonymous client logs."""
27	    rate = "10/minute"
28	
29	
30	def _contains_sensitive_key(name: str) -> bool:
31	    """Return True if *name* contains any sensitive keyword."""
32	    lower = name.lower()
33	    return any(kw in lower for kw in SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS)
34	
35	
36	def _sanitize_extra(extra: dict) -> dict:
37	    """Return a copy of *extra* with sensitive keys removed."""
38	    if not isinstance(extra, dict):
39	        return {}
40	    return {k: v for k, v in extra.items() if not _contains_sensitive_key(k)}
41	
42	
43	@api_view(["POST"])
44	@permission_classes([AllowAny])
45	@throttle_classes([ClientErrorRateThrottle])
46	def client_error_log(request):
47	    """
48	    Accept client-side JavaScript error reports.
49	    Logs them for monitoring without requiring authentication, since
50	    errors may occur on the login page or during session expiry.
51	
52	    Note: This endpoint is intentionally AllowAny to capture client-side errors
53	    from unauthenticated pages (e.g., login page errors). It is protected by:
54	    - Rate limiting (10 requests/minute per IP)
55	    - Payload schema allowlist
56	    - Sensitive field rejection
57	    - Payload size limit (10KB)
58	    - User binding when session exists
59	
60	    Expected payload:
61	    {
62	        "level": "error" | "warn" | "info",
63	        "message": "...",
64	        "stack": "...",
65	        "url": "...",
66	        "timestamp": "...",
67	        "component": "...",
68	        "extra": { ... }
69	    }
70	    """
71	    # ── 0. Origin / Referer check (exact scheme+host+port match) ────
72	    from urllib.parse import urlparse
73	    from django.conf import settings
74	    trusted_raw = getattr(settings, "CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS", [])
75	    trusted_origins = set()
76	    for t in trusted_raw:
77	        p = urlparse(t)
78	        trusted_origins.add(f"{p.scheme}://{p.netloc}")
79	    origin = request.META.get("HTTP_ORIGIN", "")
80	    referer = request.META.get("HTTP_REFERER", "")
81	    check_value = origin or referer
82	    if check_value:
83	        p = urlparse(check_value)
84	        request_origin = f"{p.scheme}://{p.netloc}"
85	    else:
86	        request_origin = ""
87	    if request_origin and request_origin not in trusted_origins:
88	        return Response(
89	            {"error": "origin_rejected", "message": "Untrusted origin.", "status_code": 403},
90	            status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
91	        )
92	    if not check_value and not (request.user and request.user.is_authenticated):
93	        # No origin/referer AND no session: reject as untraceable
94	        return Response(
95	            {"error": "origin_required", "message": "Origin or Referer header required.", "status_code": 403},
96	            status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
97	        )
98	
99	    # ── 1. Reject payloads larger than 10 KB ──────────────────────────
100	    content_length = request.META.get("CONTENT_LENGTH")
101	    if content_length is not None:
102	        try:
103	            if int(content_length) > MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES:
104	                return Response(
105	                    {
106	                        "error": "payload_too_large",
107	                        "message": f"Request body must not exceed {MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES} bytes.",
108	                        "status_code": 413,
109	                    },
110	                    status=status.HTTP_413_REQUEST_ENTITY_TOO_LARGE,
111	                )
112	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
113	            pass
114	
115	    data = request.data or {}
116	
117	    # ── 2. Reject unknown top-level fields ────────────────────────────
118	    unknown_fields = set(data.keys()) - ALLOWED_FIELDS
119	    if unknown_fields:
120	        return Response(
121	            {
122	                "error": "validation_error",
123	                "message": f"Unknown fields: {', '.join(sorted(unknown_fields))}",
124	                "status_code": 400,
125	            },
126	            status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
127	        )
128	
129	    # ── 3. Reject any top-level field whose name is sensitive ─────────
130	    for field_name in data.keys():
131	        if _contains_sensitive_key(field_name):
132	            return Response(
133	                {
134	                    "error": "validation_error",
135	                    "message": f"Sensitive field '{field_name}' is not allowed.",
136	                    "status_code": 400,
137	                },
138	                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
139	            )
140	
141	    level = data.get("level", "error")
142	    message = str(data.get("message", ""))[:MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH]
143	    stack = str(data.get("stack", ""))[:MAX_STACK_LENGTH]
144	    url = str(data.get("url", ""))[:500]
145	    user_agent = str(data.get("user_agent", ""))[:500]
146	    timestamp = str(data.get("timestamp", ""))[:100]
147	    component = str(data.get("component", ""))[:200]
148	    extra = data.get("extra", {})
149	
150	    if not message:
151	        return Response(
152	            {
153	                "error": "validation_error",
154	                "message": "The 'message' field is required.",
155	                "status_code": 400,
156	            },
157	            status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
158	        )
159	
160	    # ── 4. Sanitize the extra dict ────────────────────────────────────
161	    extra = _sanitize_extra(extra)
162	
163	    log_data = {
164	        "source": "client",
165	        "level": level,
166	        "message": message,
167	        "url": url,
168	        "user_agent": user_agent,
169	    }
170	    if stack:
171	        log_data["stack"] = stack
172	    if timestamp:
173	        log_data["timestamp"] = timestamp
174	    if component:
175	        log_data["component"] = component
176	    if extra:
177	        log_data["extra"] = extra
178	
179	    # ── 5. Bind authenticated user if present ─────────────────────────
180	    if request.user and request.user.is_authenticated:
181	        log_data["user_id"] = str(request.user.pk)
182	        log_data["username"] = request.user.username
183	
184	    log_fn = logger.error if level == "error" else (logger.warning if level == "warn" else logger.info)
185	    log_fn(
186	        "CLIENT %s: %s",
187	        level.upper(),
188	        message,
189	        extra={"client_log": log_data},
190	    )
191	
192	    return Response({"status": "logged"}, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
193

30	@api_view(["GET", "POST"])
31	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsComplianceOrAdmin])
32	def subscription_list_create(request):
33	    """
34	    GET  /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/  -- list (compliance/admin only)
35	    POST /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/  -- create (admin only)
36	    """
37	    if request.method == "GET":
38	        subscriptions = ReportSubscription.objects.select_related("created_by").all()
39	
40	        page = int(request.query_params.get("page", 1))
41	        page_size = int(request.query_params.get("page_size", 20))
42	        page_size = min(page_size, 100)
43	        start = (page - 1) * page_size
44	        end = start + page_size
45	        total = subscriptions.count()
46	
47	        serializer = ReportSubscriptionListSerializer(
48	            subscriptions[start:end], many=True
49	        )
50	        return Response({
51	            "count": total,
52	            "next": None,
53	            "previous": None,
54	            "results": serializer.data,
55	        })
56	
57	    # POST -- admin only.
58	    if not IsAdmin().has_permission(request, None):
59	        return Response(
60	            {
61	                "error": "permission_denied",
62	                "message": "Administrator privileges required to create subscriptions.",
63	                "status_code": 403,
64	            },
65	            status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
66	        )
67	
68	    serializer = ReportSubscriptionCreateSerializer(data=request.data)
69	    serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)
70	    subscription = serializer.save(created_by=request.user)
71	
72	    request._audit_context = {
73	        "event_type": "create",
74	        "target_model": "ReportSubscription",
75	        "target_id": str(subscription.pk),
76	        "target_repr": subscription.name,
77	    }
78	
79	    return Response(
80	        ReportSubscriptionListSerializer(subscription).data,
81	        status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
82	    )

85	@api_view(["GET", "PATCH", "DELETE"])
86	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsComplianceOrAdmin])
87	def subscription_detail(request, pk):
88	    """
89	    GET    /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/{id}/
90	    PATCH  /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/{id}/  (admin or creator only)
91	    DELETE /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/{id}/  (admin or creator only -- soft deactivate)
92	    """
93	    try:
94	        subscription = ReportSubscription.objects.select_related("created_by").get(pk=pk)
95	    except ReportSubscription.DoesNotExist:
96	        return Response(
97	            {
98	                "error": "not_found",
99	                "message": "Report subscription not found.",
100	                "status_code": 404,
101	            },
102	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
103	        )
104	
105	    if request.method == "GET":
106	        return Response(ReportSubscriptionListSerializer(subscription).data)
107	
108	    # PATCH / DELETE require admin or the creator of the subscription.
109	    is_admin = request.user.role == "admin"
110	    is_creator = (
111	        subscription.created_by_id is not None
112	        and subscription.created_by_id == request.user.pk
113	    )
114	    if not (is_admin or is_creator):
115	        return Response(
116	            {
117	                "error": "permission_denied",
118	                "message": "Administrator privileges or subscription ownership required.",
119	                "status_code": 403,
120	            },
121	            status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
122	        )
123	
124	    if request.method == "PATCH":
125	        serializer = ReportSubscriptionCreateSerializer(
126	            subscription, data=request.data, partial=True
127	        )
128	        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)
129	        serializer.save()
130	
131	        request._audit_context = {
132	            "event_type": "update",
133	            "target_model": "ReportSubscription",
134	            "target_id": str(subscription.pk),
135	            "target_repr": subscription.name,
136	            "field_changes": request.data,
137	        }
138	
139	        return Response(ReportSubscriptionListSerializer(subscription).data)
140	
141	    # DELETE -- soft-deactivate.
142	    subscription.is_active = False
143	    subscription.save(update_fields=["is_active", "updated_at"])
144	
145	    request._audit_context = {
146	        "event_type": "update",
147	        "target_model": "ReportSubscription",
148	        "target_id": str(subscription.pk),
149	        "target_repr": f"Deactivated: {subscription.name}",
150	    }
151	
152	    return Response({"message": "Subscription deactivated."})
153

155	@api_view(["POST"])
156	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsAdmin])
157	def subscription_run_now(request, pk):
158	    """
159	    POST /api/v1/reports/subscriptions/{id}/run-now/
160	
161	    Trigger immediate report generation. Admin only.
162	    """
163	    try:
164	        subscription = ReportSubscription.objects.get(pk=pk)
165	    except ReportSubscription.DoesNotExist:
166	        return Response(
167	            {
168	                "error": "not_found",
169	                "message": "Report subscription not found.",
170	                "status_code": 404,
171	            },
172	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
173	        )
174	
175	    # Determine file format based on output_format.
176	    format_map = {"pdf": "pdf", "excel": "xlsx", "image": "png"}
177	    file_format = format_map.get(subscription.output_format, "pdf")
178	
179	    outbox_item = OutboxItem.objects.create(
180	        subscription=subscription,
181	        report_name=f"{subscription.name} (manual run)",
182	        file_format=file_format,
183	        status=OutboxStatus.QUEUED,
184	        delivery_target=subscription.parameters.get("delivery_target", "shared_folder"),
185	        delivery_target_path=subscription.parameters.get("delivery_path", ""),
186	    )
187	
188	    # Trigger async generation.
189	    try:
190	        from .tasks import generate_report
191	        generate_report.delay(str(outbox_item.pk))
192	    except Exception:
193	        logger.exception("Failed to enqueue generate_report task")
194	
195	    request._audit_context = {
196	        "event_type": "report_generated",
197	        "target_model": "ReportSubscription",
198	        "target_id": str(subscription.pk),
199	        "target_repr": f"Manual run: {subscription.name}",
200	        "extra_data": {"outbox_item_id": str(outbox_item.pk)},
201	    }
202	
203	    return Response(
204	        {
205	            "message": "Report generation queued.",
206	            "outbox_item": OutboxItemSerializer(outbox_item).data,
207	        },
208	        status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
209	    )

275	@api_view(["GET"])
276	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsComplianceOrAdmin])
277	def outbox_download(request, pk):
278	    """
279	    GET /api/v1/reports/outbox/{id}/download/
280	
281	    Serve the generated report file for download.
282	    Admin and compliance only.
283	    """
284	    try:
285	        item = OutboxItem.objects.get(pk=pk)
286	    except OutboxItem.DoesNotExist:
287	        return Response(
288	            {
289	                "error": "not_found",
290	                "message": "Outbox item not found.",
291	                "status_code": 404,
292	            },
293	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
294	        )
295	
296	    if not item.file_path:
297	        return Response(
298	            {
299	                "error": "file_not_ready",
300	                "message": "Report file has not been generated yet.",
301	                "status_code": 404,
302	            },
303	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
304	        )
305	
306	    storage_root = getattr(settings, "MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT", settings.MEDIA_ROOT)
307	    full_path = os.path.join(storage_root, item.file_path)
308	
309	    if not os.path.isfile(full_path):
310	        return Response(
311	            {
312	                "error": "file_missing",
313	                "message": "Report file not found on disk.",
314	                "status_code": 404,
315	            },
316	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
317	        )
318	
319	    content_types = {
320	        "pdf": "application/pdf",
321	        "xlsx": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet",
322	        "png": "image/png",
323	    }
324	    content_type = content_types.get(item.file_format, "application/octet-stream")
325	    filename = os.path.basename(full_path)
326	
327	    return FileResponse(
328	        open(full_path, "rb"),
329	        content_type=content_type,
330	        as_attachment=True,
331	        filename=filename,
332	    )

360	                "status_code": 400,
361	            },

381	        "target_repr": f"Manual retry: {item.report_name}",
382	    }

441	@api_view(["GET"])
442	@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated, IsComplianceOrAdmin])
443	def report_dashboard(request):
444	    """
445	    GET /api/v1/reports/dashboard/
446	
447	    Counts by outbox status plus recent items.
448	    Admin and compliance only.
449	    """
450	    from django.db.models import Count, Q
451	
452	    aggregates = OutboxItem.objects.aggregate(
453	        queued=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.QUEUED)),
454	        generating=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.GENERATING)),
455	        delivered=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.DELIVERED)),
456	        failed=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.FAILED)),
457	        stalled=Count("id", filter=Q(status=OutboxStatus.STALLED)),
458	        total=Count("id"),
459	    )
460	
461	    recent_items = OutboxItem.objects.select_related(
462	        "subscription", "acknowledged_by"
463	    ).order_by("-generated_at")[:10]
464	
465	    dashboard_serializer = OutboxDashboardSerializer(aggregates)
466	
467	    return Response({
468	        "queued": aggregates["queued"],
469	        "generating": aggregates["generating"],
470	        "delivered": aggregates["delivered"],
471	        "failed": aggregates["failed"],
472	        "stalled": aggregates["stalled"],
473	        "recent": OutboxItemSerializer(recent_items, many=True).data,

1	"""
2	Pure-Python consent domain service.
3	No Django ORM imports -- works with plain values / dicts.
4	"""
5	from datetime import date, datetime
6	
7	from domain.exceptions import ConflictError, ValidationError
8	
9	
10	def compute_consent_status(
11	    *,
12	    is_revoked: bool,
13	    revoked_at: datetime | None = None,
14	    expiration_date: date | None = None,
15	    effective_date: date | None = None,
16	) -> str:
17	    """
18	    Determine the current status of a consent record.
19	
20	    Returns one of: "active", "expired", "revoked".
21	    """
22	    if is_revoked:
23	        return "revoked"
24	
25	    today = date.today()
26	
27	    if expiration_date and expiration_date < today:
28	        return "expired"
29	
30	    if effective_date and effective_date > today:
31	        # Not yet effective -- still considered "active" (pending start)
32	        return "active"
33	
34	    return "active"
35	
36	
37	def validate_consent_dates(
38	    effective_date: date,
39	    expiration_date: date | None,
40	) -> None:
41	    """
42	    Validate that the expiration date (if provided) is after the
43	    effective date.
44	
45	    Raises domain.exceptions.ValidationError on failure.
46	    """
47	    if expiration_date is not None and expiration_date <= effective_date:
48	        raise ValidationError(
49	            "Expiration date must be after the effective date."
50	        )
51	
52	
53	def validate_revocation(
54	    *,
55	    is_revoked: bool,
56	    physical_copy_on_file: bool,
57	    acknowledged_warning: bool,
58	) -> None:
59	    """
60	    Validate that a consent can be revoked.
61	
62	    Raises ConflictError if already revoked.
63	    Raises ValidationError if a physical-copy warning acknowledgement is
64	    required but was not provided.
65	    """
66	    if is_revoked:
67	        raise ConflictError("This consent has already been revoked.")
68	
69	    if physical_copy_on_file and not acknowledged_warning:
70	        raise ValidationError(
71	            "A physical copy of this consent is on file. "
72	            "You must acknowledge the physical_copy_warning before revoking."
73	        )
74

1	"""Consent models: Consent and ConsentScope."""
2	import uuid
3	from datetime import date

5	from django.conf import settings
6	from django.db import models
7	
8	from domain.services.consent_service import compute_consent_status

11	class Consent(models.Model):
12	    """
13	    Patient consent record tracking authorisation for data use,
14	    treatment, media use, or third-party sharing.
15	    """
16	
17	    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
18	    patient = models.ForeignKey(
19	        "mpi.Patient",
20	        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
21	        related_name="consents",
22	    )
23	    purpose = models.CharField(max_length=200)
24	
25	    # Who granted it
26	    granted_by = models.ForeignKey(
27	        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
28	        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
29	        related_name="granted_consents",
30	    )
31	    granted_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
32	
33	    # Date range
34	    effective_date = models.DateField()
35	    expiration_date = models.DateField(
36	        null=True,
37	        blank=True,
38	        help_text="Null means the consent is indefinite.",
39	    )
40	
41	    # Revocation
42	    is_revoked = models.BooleanField(default=False)
43	    revoked_at = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
44	    revoked_by = models.ForeignKey(
45	        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
46	        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
47	        null=True,
48	        blank=True,
49	        related_name="revoked_consents",
50	    )
51	    revocation_reason = models.TextField(blank=True, default="")
52	
53	    # Physical copy tracking
54	    physical_copy_on_file = models.BooleanField(default=False)
55	    physical_copy_warning_shown = models.BooleanField(default=False)
56	
57	    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
58	    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
59	
60	    class Meta:
61	        db_table = "consents"
62	        ordering = ["-granted_at"]
63	
64	    def __str__(self) -> str:
65	        return f"Consent {self.id} ({self.purpose}) for patient {self.patient_id}"
66	
67	    @property
68	    def status(self) -> str:
69	        """Computed status: 'active', 'expired', or 'revoked'."""
70	        return compute_consent_status(
71	            is_revoked=self.is_revoked,
72	            revoked_at=self.revoked_at,
73	            expiration_date=self.expiration_date,
74	            effective_date=self.effective_date,
75	        )
76

100	    class Meta:

31	class ConsentCreateSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
32	    """Create a new consent record for a patient."""
33	
34	    purpose = serializers.CharField(max_length=200)
35	    effective_date = serializers.DateField()
36	    expiration_date = serializers.DateField(required=False, allow_null=True)
37	    physical_copy_on_file = serializers.BooleanField(required=False, default=False)
38	    scopes = ConsentScopeSerializer(many=True, required=False)
39	
40	    def validate(self, data):
41	        effective = data["effective_date"]
42	        expiration = data.get("expiration_date")
43	
44	        try:
45	            validate_consent_dates(effective, expiration)
46	        except DomainValidationError as exc:
47	            raise serializers.ValidationError({"expiration_date": str(exc)})
48	
49	        return data
50	
51	    def create(self, validated_data):
52	        scopes_data = validated_data.pop("scopes", [])
53	        patient = self.context["patient"]
54	        user = self.context["request"].user
55	
56	        consent = Consent.objects.create(
57	            patient=patient,
58	            granted_by=user,
59	            purpose=validated_data["purpose"],
60	            effective_date=validated_data["effective_date"],
61	            expiration_date=validated_data.get("expiration_date"),
62	            physical_copy_on_file=validated_data.get("physical_copy_on_file", False),
63	        )
64	
65	        for scope in scopes_data:
66	            ConsentScope.objects.create(consent=consent, **scope)
67	
68	        logger.info(
69	            "Consent created: id=%s patient=%s purpose=%s",
70	            consent.id,
71	            patient.id,
72	            consent.purpose,
73	        )
74	        return consent

89	            "purpose": instance.purpose,
90	            "status": instance.status,
91	            "granted_by": str(instance.granted_by_id),
92	            "granted_at": instance.granted_at.isoformat() if instance.granted_at else None,
93	            "effective_date": str(instance.effective_date),
94	            "expiration_date": str(instance.expiration_date) if instance.expiration_date else None,
95	            "is_revoked": instance.is_revoked,
96	            "revoked_at": instance.revoked_at.isoformat() if instance.revoked_at else None,
97	            "revoked_by": str(instance.revoked_by_id) if instance.revoked_by_id else None,
98	            "revocation_reason": instance.revocation_reason,
99	            "physical_copy_on_file": instance.physical_copy_on_file,
100	            "scopes": ConsentScopeSerializer(instance.scopes.all(), many=True).data,
101	            "created_at": instance.created_at.isoformat() if instance.created_at else None,
102	            "updated_at": instance.updated_at.isoformat() if instance.updated_at else None,
103	        }
104	
105	
106	# ── ConsentRevokeSerializer ─────────────────────────────────────────
107	
108	class ConsentRevokeSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
109	    """Revoke an existing consent."""
110	
111	    reason = serializers.CharField(min_length=1)
112	    physical_copy_warning_acknowledged = serializers.BooleanField(
113	        required=False, default=False,
114	    )
115	
116	    def validate(self, data):
117	        consent = self.context["consent"]
118	
119	        try:
120	            validate_revocation(
121	                is_revoked=consent.is_revoked,
122	                physical_copy_on_file=consent.physical_copy_on_file,
123	                acknowledged_warning=data.get("physical_copy_warning_acknowledged", False),
124	            )
125	        except ConflictError as exc:
126	            raise serializers.ValidationError({"non_field_errors": [str(exc)]})
127	        except DomainValidationError as exc:
128	            raise serializers.ValidationError(
129	                {"physical_copy_warning_acknowledged": str(exc)}
130	            )
131	
132	        return data
133	
134	    def save(self):
135	        consent = self.context["consent"]
136	        user = self.context["request"].user
137	
138	        consent.is_revoked = True
139	        consent.revoked_at = timezone.now()
140	        consent.revoked_by = user
141	        consent.revocation_reason = self.validated_data["reason"]
142	
143	        if consent.physical_copy_on_file:
144	            consent.physical_copy_warning_shown = True
145	
146	        consent.save()
147	
148	        logger.info(
149	            "Consent revoked: id=%s patient=%s by=%s",
150	            consent.id,
151	            consent.patient_id,
152	            user.id,
153	        )
154	        return consent
155

14	def validate_infringement_transition(old_status: str, new_status: str) -> bool:
15	    """
16	    Return True if the transition from *old_status* to *new_status* is
17	    allowed, False otherwise.
18	    """

20	    return new_status in allowed

1	"""
2	Media processing service: hashing, originality checks, watermarking,
3	file-type validation, and EXIF metadata extraction.
4	"""
5	import hashlib
6	import logging
7	import math
8	
9	from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont, ExifTags
10	
11	# Cap decompression bomb threshold before any image is opened.
12	Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS = 25_000_000
13	
14	logger = logging.getLogger("medrights.media")
15	
16	# MIME types accepted by the system (matched via python-magic).
17	ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES = {
18	    "image/jpeg",
19	    "image/png",
20	}
21	
22	
23	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	# Pixel hash
25	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	def compute_pixel_hash(file_obj) -> str:
28	    """
29	    Open the image with Pillow, load raw pixel data (stripping EXIF),
30	    and return the SHA-256 hex digest of Image.tobytes().
31	
32	    Raises ValueError for corrupt / unreadable images.
33	    """
34	    try:
35	        file_obj.seek(0)
36	        img = Image.open(file_obj)
37	        img.load()  # force full decode, strips lazy loading
38	    except Exception as exc:
39	        raise ValueError(
40	            f"Unable to decode image file: {exc}"
41	        ) from exc
42	
43	    # Convert to RGB to normalise colour space (handles RGBA, P, etc.)
44	    img = img.convert("RGB")
45	
46	    pixel_data = img.tobytes()
47	    return hashlib.sha256(pixel_data).hexdigest()
48	
49	
50	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	# File hash
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	def compute_file_hash(file_obj) -> str:
55	    """Return the SHA-256 hex digest of the entire file contents."""
56	    file_obj.seek(0)
57	    h = hashlib.sha256()
58	    while True:
59	        chunk = file_obj.read(65_536)
60	        if not chunk:
61	            break
62	        if isinstance(chunk, str):
63	            chunk = chunk.encode("utf-8")
64	        h.update(chunk)
65	    file_obj.seek(0)
66	    return h.hexdigest()
67	
68	
69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	# Originality check
71	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
72	
73	def check_originality(pixel_hash: str):
74	    """
75	    Query MediaAsset by pixel_hash.
76	
77	    Returns:
78	        (status, matching_asset_or_None)
79	        - ("original", None) when no match is found
80	        - ("reposted", <MediaAsset>) when a match exists
81	    """
82	    from apps.media_engine.models import MediaAsset
83	
84	    existing = (
85	        MediaAsset.objects
86	        .filter(pixel_hash=pixel_hash, is_deleted=False)
87	        .first()
88	    )
89	    if existing:
90	        return ("reposted", existing)
91	    return ("original", None)

94	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
95	# Watermark
96	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
97	
98	def apply_watermark(image_path: str, output_path: str, config: dict) -> str:
99	    """
100	    Burn a diagonal text watermark onto the image at *image_path* and
101	    write the result to *output_path*.
102	
103	    config keys:
104	        clinic_name : str   -- text line 1
105	        date_stamp  : bool  -- if True, append today's date
106	        opacity     : float -- 0.0 .. 1.0
107	
108	    Returns the output_path on success.
109	    """
110	    from datetime import date as _date
111	
112	    img = Image.open(image_path).convert("RGBA")
113	    width, height = img.size
114	
115	    # Build watermark text
116	    text = config.get("clinic_name", "")
117	    if config.get("date_stamp"):
118	        text += f"  {_date.today().isoformat()}"
119	
120	    # Create transparent overlay
121	    overlay = Image.new("RGBA", img.size, (0, 0, 0, 0))
122	    draw = ImageDraw.Draw(overlay)
123	
124	    # Attempt to load a reasonable font size; fall back to default
125	    font_size = max(20, min(width, height) // 20)
126	    try:
127	        font = ImageFont.truetype("arial.ttf", font_size)
128	    except (IOError, OSError):
129	        try:
130	            font = ImageFont.truetype("/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSans.ttf", font_size)
131	        except (IOError, OSError):
132	            font = ImageFont.load_default()
133	
134	    # Measure text for tiling
135	    bbox = draw.textbbox((0, 0), text, font=font)
136	    text_width = bbox[2] - bbox[0]
137	    text_height = bbox[3] - bbox[1]
138	
139	    if text_width == 0 or text_height == 0:
140	        # Nothing to draw
141	        img.save(output_path)
142	        return output_path
143	
144	    # Compute opacity as an alpha value 0-255
145	    alpha = int(255 * max(0.0, min(1.0, config.get("opacity", 0.35))))
146	
147	    # Create a single text tile rotated -45 degrees
148	    diagonal = int(math.sqrt(width ** 2 + height ** 2))
149	    txt_layer = Image.new("RGBA", (diagonal, diagonal), (0, 0, 0, 0))
150	    txt_draw = ImageDraw.Draw(txt_layer)
151	
152	    # Tile the text across the diagonal canvas
153	    y = 0
154	    spacing_y = text_height * 3
155	    spacing_x = text_width + 60
156	    while y < diagonal:
157	        x = 0
158	        while x < diagonal:
159	            txt_draw.text((x, y), text, font=font, fill=(255, 255, 255, alpha))
160	            x += spacing_x
161	        y += spacing_y
162	
163	    # Rotate and crop to original size
164	    txt_layer = txt_layer.rotate(45, resample=Image.BICUBIC, expand=False)
165	
166	    # Center-crop the rotated overlay to match original image size
167	    cx, cy = txt_layer.size[0] // 2, txt_layer.size[1] // 2
168	    left = cx - width // 2
169	    top = cy - height // 2
170	    txt_layer = txt_layer.crop((left, top, left + width, top + height))
171	
172	    # Composite
173	    result = Image.alpha_composite(img, txt_layer)
174	    result = result.convert("RGB")
175	    result.save(output_path)
176	    return output_path
177	
178	
179	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	# File-type validation via magic bytes
181	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
182	
183	def validate_file_type(file_obj) -> str:
184	    """
185	    Check magic bytes of *file_obj* using python-magic.
186	
187	    Returns the detected MIME type if it is JPEG or PNG.
188	    Raises ValueError for anything else.
189	    """
190	    import magic
191	
192	    file_obj.seek(0)
193	    header = file_obj.read(8192)
194	    file_obj.seek(0)
195	
196	    detected = magic.from_buffer(header, mime=True)
197	
198	    if detected not in ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES:
199	        raise ValueError(
200	            f"Unsupported file type '{detected}'. Only JPEG and PNG are accepted."
201	        )
202	
203	    return detected
204	
205	
206	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
207	# Evidence metadata extraction
208	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
209	
210	def extract_evidence_metadata(file_obj) -> dict:
211	    """
212	    Extract available EXIF / image metadata into a plain dict.
213	    This is stored immutably at upload time.
214	    """
215	    metadata: dict = {}
216	
217	    try:
218	        file_obj.seek(0)
219	        img = Image.open(file_obj)
220	
221	        metadata["width"] = img.width
222	        metadata["height"] = img.height
223	        metadata["format"] = img.format
224	        metadata["mode"] = img.mode
225	
226	        exif_data = img.getexif()
227	        if exif_data:
228	            tag_map = {v: k for k, v in ExifTags.TAGS.items()}
229	
230	            for tag_name in (
231	                "Make",
232	                "Model",
233	                "DateTime",
234	                "DateTimeOriginal",
235	                "Software",
236	                "ImageWidth",
237	                "ImageLength",
238	                "ExifImageWidth",
239	                "ExifImageHeight",
240	                "Orientation",
241	                "XResolution",
242	                "YResolution",
243	            ):
244	                tag_id = tag_map.get(tag_name)
245	                if tag_id is not None and tag_id in exif_data:
246	                    value = exif_data[tag_id]
247	                    # Convert IFDRational / tuple to string for JSON serialisation
248	                    if hasattr(value, "numerator"):
249	                        value = float(value)
250	                    elif isinstance(value, bytes):
251	                        value = value.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
252	                    elif isinstance(value, tuple):
253	                        value = str(value)
254	                    metadata[f"exif_{tag_name}"] = value
255	
256	    except Exception:
257	        # If we cannot read metadata that is not a fatal error;
258	        # the image itself was already validated earlier.
259	        logger.debug("Could not extract EXIF metadata", exc_info=True)
260	
261	    file_obj.seek(0)
262	    return metadata

52	@api_view(["POST"])

54	def media_upload(request):
55	    """
56	    POST /api/v1/media/upload/
57	    Upload a media file with fingerprinting and originality check.

60	        data=request.data,
61	        context={"request": request},

64	
65	    try:

67	    except Exception as exc:
68	        logger.exception("Media upload failed")
69	        return Response(

71	                "error": "upload_failed",

75	            status=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,

78	    request._audit_context = {
79	        "event_type": "media_upload",
80	        "target_model": "MediaAsset",
81	        "target_id": str(asset.pk),
82	        "target_repr": asset.original_filename,
83	        "extra_data": {

99	@api_view(["GET"])
100	@permission_classes([IsFrontDeskOrClinicianOrAdmin])
101	def media_list(request):
102	    """
103	    GET /api/v1/media/
104	    List media assets with optional filters: patient_id, originality_status.
105	    """
106	    qs = MediaAsset.objects.filter(is_deleted=False).select_related("patient")
107	
108	    patient_id = request.query_params.get("patient_id")
109	    if patient_id:
110	        qs = qs.filter(patient_id=patient_id)
111	
112	    originality_status = request.query_params.get("originality_status")
113	    if originality_status:
114	        qs = qs.filter(originality_status=originality_status)
115	
116	    search = request.query_params.get("search", "").strip()
117	    if search:
118	        qs = qs.filter(original_filename__icontains=search)
119	
120	    # Simple offset/limit pagination
121	    page = int(request.query_params.get("page", 1))
122	    page_size = int(request.query_params.get("page_size", 20))
123	    start = (page - 1) * page_size
124	    end = start + page_size
125	
126	    total = qs.count()
127	    assets = qs[start:end]
128	
129	    return Response({
130	        "count": total,
131	        "page": page,
132	        "page_size": page_size,
133	        "results": MediaListSerializer(assets, many=True).data,
134	    })

168	
169	@api_view(["GET"])
170	@permission_classes([IsFrontDeskOrClinicianOrAdmin])
171	def media_download(request, pk):
172	    """
173	    GET /api/v1/media/{id}/download/
174	    Serve the original file with proper content type.
175	    Checks that consent is not revoked before serving.
176	    """
177	    try:
178	        asset = MediaAsset.objects.select_related("consent").get(
179	            pk=pk, is_deleted=False
180	        )
181	    except MediaAsset.DoesNotExist:
182	        return Response(
183	            {
184	                "error": "not_found",
185	                "message": "Media asset not found.",
186	                "status_code": 404,
187	            },
188	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
189	        )
190	
191	    # Check consent status if the asset is linked to a consent record
192	    if asset.consent is not None:
193	        is_revoked = getattr(asset.consent, "is_revoked", False)
194	        if is_revoked:
195	            return Response(
196	                {
197	                    "error": "consent_revoked",
198	                    "message": "The consent for this media has been revoked. Download is not permitted.",
199	                    "status_code": 403,
200	                },
201	                status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
202	            )
203	
204	    # Check repost authorization: reposted media must have a valid citation
205	    if asset.originality_status == "reposted":
206	        has_authorization = Citation.objects.filter(
207	            media_asset=asset,
208	            citation_text__isnull=False,
209	            authorization_file_path__isnull=False,
210	        ).exclude(
211	            citation_text="",
212	        ).exclude(
213	            authorization_file_path="",
214	        ).exists()
215	
216	        if not has_authorization:
217	            return Response(
218	                {
219	                    "error": "repost_not_authorized",
220	                    "message": "This reposted media requires citation and authorization before download.",
221	                    "status_code": 403,
222	                },
223	                status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
224	            )
225	
226	    absolute_path = os.path.join(
227	        settings.MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT, asset.original_file
228	    )
229	
230	    if not os.path.isfile(absolute_path):
231	        return Response(
232	            {
233	                "error": "file_missing",
234	                "message": "The file is no longer available on disk.",
235	                "status_code": 404,
236	            },
237	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
238	        )
239	
240	    request._audit_context = {
241	        "event_type": "media_download",
242	        "target_model": "MediaAsset",
243	        "target_id": str(asset.pk),
244	        "target_repr": asset.original_filename,
245	    }
246	
247	    response = FileResponse(
248	        open(absolute_path, "rb"),
249	        content_type=asset.mime_type,
250	    )
251	    response["Content-Disposition"] = (
252	        f'attachment; filename="{asset.original_filename}"'
253	    )
254	    return response

260	
261	@api_view(["POST"])
262	@permission_classes([IsFrontDeskOrClinicianOrAdmin])
263	def media_watermark(request, pk):
264	    """
265	    POST /api/v1/media/{id}/watermark/
266	    Apply a server-side watermark burn to the media asset.
267	    """
268	    try:
269	        asset = MediaAsset.objects.get(pk=pk, is_deleted=False)
270	    except MediaAsset.DoesNotExist:
271	        return Response(
272	            {
273	                "error": "not_found",
274	                "message": "Media asset not found.",
275	                "status_code": 404,
276	            },
277	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
278	        )
279	
280	    serializer = WatermarkSerializer(data=request.data)
281	    serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)
282	    config = serializer.validated_data
283	
284	    source_path = os.path.join(
285	        settings.MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT, asset.original_file
286	    )
287	
288	    if not os.path.isfile(source_path):
289	        return Response(
290	            {
291	                "error": "file_missing",
292	                "message": "The original file is no longer available on disk.",
293	                "status_code": 404,
294	            },
295	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
296	        )
297	
298	    # Build watermarked output path
299	    today = date.today()
300	    rel_dir = os.path.join(
301	        "watermarked", str(today.year), f"{today.month:02d}"
302	    )
303	    abs_dir = os.path.join(settings.MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT, rel_dir)
304	    os.makedirs(abs_dir, exist_ok=True)
305	
306	    ext = os.path.splitext(asset.original_filename)[1] or ".jpg"
307	    out_name = f"{uuid.uuid4().hex}{ext}"
308	    relative_output = os.path.join(rel_dir, out_name)
309	    absolute_output = os.path.join(settings.MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT, relative_output)
310	
311	    with transaction.atomic():
312	        try:
313	            apply_watermark(source_path, absolute_output, config)
314	        except Exception as exc:
315	            logger.exception("Watermark application failed for asset %s", pk)
316	            return Response(
317	                {
318	                    "error": "watermark_failed",
319	                    "message": "Watermark could not be applied. Please verify the source image and try again.",
320	                    "status_code": 422,
321	                },
322	                status=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
323	            )
324	
325	        # Compute derivative hash
326	        with open(absolute_output, "rb") as f:
327	            derivative_hash = compute_file_hash(f)
328	
329	        # Create derivative record
330	        MediaDerivative.objects.create(
331	            parent_asset=asset,
332	            derivative_path=relative_output,
333	            derivative_hash=derivative_hash,
334	            watermark_applied=True,
335	            watermark_settings=config,
336	            created_by=request.user,
337	        )
338	
339	        # Update the parent asset
340	        asset.watermarked_file = relative_output
341	        asset.watermark_settings = config
342	        asset.watermark_burned = True
343	        asset.save(update_fields=[
344	            "watermarked_file",
345	            "watermark_settings",
346	            "watermark_burned",
347	            "updated_at",
348	        ])
349	
350	    request._audit_context = {
351	        "event_type": "media_watermark",
352	        "target_model": "MediaAsset",
353	        "target_id": str(asset.pk),
354	        "target_repr": asset.original_filename,
355	        "extra_data": {"watermark_config": config},
356	    }
357	
358	    return Response(MediaDetailSerializer(asset).data)
359

369	    POST /api/v1/media/{id}/repost/authorize/

371	    """

373	        asset = MediaAsset.objects.get(pk=pk, is_deleted=False)

400	    auth_absolute = os.path.join(settings.MEDRIGHTS_STORAGE_ROOT, auth_relative)

409	        authorization_file_path=auth_relative,

412	    )

414	    # Transition originality status to "reposted_authorized"
415	    if asset.originality_status == "reposted":
416	        asset.originality_status = "reposted_authorized"
417	        asset.save(update_fields=["originality_status"])
418	
419	    request._audit_context = {
420	        "event_type": "media_repost_authorize",
421	        "target_model": "MediaAsset",
422	        "target_id": str(asset.pk),
423	        "target_repr": asset.original_filename,
424	        "extra_data": {"citation_id": str(citation.pk)},
425	    }
426	
427	    return Response(
428	        CitationSerializer(citation).data,
429	        status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
430	    )

437	@api_view(["POST"])
438	@permission_classes([IsFrontDeskOrClinicianOrAdmin])
439	def media_attach_patient(request, pk):
440	    """
441	    POST /api/v1/media/{id}/attach-patient/
442	    Link a media asset to a patient.  Accepts ``{ "patient_id": "<uuid>" }``.
443	    """
444	    from apps.mpi.models import Patient
445	
446	    try:
447	        asset = MediaAsset.objects.get(pk=pk, is_deleted=False)
448	    except MediaAsset.DoesNotExist:
449	        return Response(
450	            {
451	                "error": "not_found",
452	                "message": "Media asset not found.",
453	                "status_code": 404,
454	            },
455	            status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
456	        )
457	
458	    patient_id = request.data.get("patient_id")
459	    if not patient_id:
460	        return Response(
461	            {
462	                "error": "validation_error",
463	                "message": "patient_id is required.",
464	                "status_code": 400,
465	            },
466	            status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
467	        )
468	
469	    # Approval gate: reject disputed media or unauthorized reposts
470	    if asset.originality_status == "disputed":
471	        return Response(
472	            {
473	                "error": "media_not_approved",
474	                "message": "Disputed media cannot be attached to patient materials.",
475	                "status_code": 403,
476	            },
477	            status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
478	        )
479	    if asset.originality_status == "reposted":
480	        from .models import Citation

- `validate_revocation()` - Validates that a consent can be revoked, checking for already-revoked state and physical copy warning acknowledgement

text.text:
Excellent! Now let me compile all the findings for you. Here's a comprehensive summary of the files and their contents:

## Summary of Findings

I've located and read all the requested files. Here's what I found:

### 1. **backend/domain/services/consent_service.py** - Full Contents

This pure-Python consent domain service (no Django dependencies) contains:

**Functions:**
- `compute_consent_status()` - Determines current status: "active", "expired", or "revoked" based on revocation, expiration, and effective dates
- `validate_consent_dates()` - Validates that expiration_date (if provided) is after effective_date
- `validate_revocation()` - Validates that a consent can be revoked, checking for already-revoked state and physical copy warning acknowledgement

### 2. **Consent Validation Helpers** - Found in Multiple Locations

**In backend/apps/consent/serializers.py:**
- `ConsentCreateSerializer.validate()` - Calls `validate_consent_dates()` for date validation
- `ConsentRevokeSerializer.validate()` - Calls `validate_revocation()` for revocation validation

**Key Finding:** There is **NO** `validate_consent_for_media_use()` function currently in the codebase. However, consent validation for media use happens in:
- `backend/apps/media_engine/views.py` at lines 192-202 in `media_download()` endpoint - checks if `asset.consent.is_revoked` before serving the file

### 3. **backend/apps/media_engine/models.py** - MediaAsset Model Definition

The MediaAsset model (lines 8-66) has a consent FK defined at lines 29-35:
```python
consent = models.ForeignKey(
    "consent.Consent",
    on_delete=models.PROTECT,
    null=True,
    blank=True,
    related_name="media_assets",
)
```

The consent field is optional (null=True, blank=True) and uses PROTECT to prevent deletion of consents that have media assets linked to them.

### 4. **backend/domain/validators/** Directory

The `/backend/domain/validators/` directory exists but is currently **empty** (only contains `__init__.py` with no content). This appears to be a placeholder for future validators.

### Key Integration Points Found:

1. **Media Upload Serializer** (`backend/apps/media_engine/serializers.py`, lines 37-139):
   - Accepts optional `consent_id` parameter
   - Validates consent exists before linking to media asset

2. **Media Download Endpoint** (`backend/apps/media_engine/views.py`, lines 169-254):
   - Checks `asset.consent.is_revoked` status
   - Returns 403 if consent is revoked with message: "The consent for this media has been revoked. Download is not permitted."

3. **Consent Models** (`backend/apps/consent/models.py`):
   - `Consent` model includes status property using `compute_consent_status()`
   - `ConsentScope` model for fine-grained scopes including "media_use" type

### Files with Consent References:
- backend/tests/integration/test_consent_api.py - Integration tests for consent API
- backend/tests/unit/test_domain_services.py - Unit tests for consent domain service
- frontend/src/api/endpoints/consents.ts - Frontend API integration
- e2e/tests/consent.spec.ts - End-to-end tests

1	"""
2	Pure-Python consent domain service.
3	No Django ORM imports -- works with plain values / dicts.
4	"""
5	from datetime import date, datetime
6	
7	from domain.exceptions import ConflictError, ValidationError
8	
9	
10	def compute_consent_status(
11	    *,
12	    is_revoked: bool,
13	    revoked_at: datetime | None = None,
14	    expiration_date: date | None = None,
15	    effective_date: date | None = None,
16	) -> str:
17	    """
18	    Determine the current status of a consent record.
19	
20	    Returns one of: "active", "expired", "revoked".
21	    """
22	    if is_revoked:
23	        return "revoked"
24	
25	    today = date.today()
26	
27	    if expiration_date and expiration_date < today:
28	        return "expired"
29	
30	    if effective_date and effective_date > today:
31	        # Not yet effective -- still considered "active" (pending start)
32	        return "active"
33	
34	    return "active"
35	
36	
37	def validate_consent_dates(
38	    effective_date: date,
39	    expiration_date: date | None,
40	) -> None:
41	    """
42	    Validate that the expiration date (if provided) is after the
43	    effective date.
44	
45	    Raises domain.exceptions.ValidationError on failure.
46	    """
47	    if expiration_date is not None and expiration_date <= effective_date:
48	        raise ValidationError(
49	            "Expiration date must be after the effective date."
50	        )
51	
52	
53	def validate_revocation(
54	    *,
55	    is_revoked: bool,
56	    physical_copy_on_file: bool,
57	    acknowledged_warning: bool,
58	) -> None:
59	    """
60	    Validate that a consent can be revoked.
61	
62	    Raises ConflictError if already revoked.
63	    Raises ValidationError if a physical-copy warning acknowledgement is
64	    required but was not provided.
65	    """
66	    if is_revoked:
67	        raise ConflictError("This consent has already been revoked.")
68	
69	    if physical_copy_on_file and not acknowledged_warning:
70	        raise ValidationError(
71	            "A physical copy of this consent is on file. "
72	            "You must acknowledge the physical_copy_warning before revoking."
73	        )
74
